steps.title: Recognize Cues
steps.sub: What matters at this adolescent well-care + sports physical visit?
steps.inst: Select ALL findings that are clinically significant cues requiring nursing attention.

opts.text: PHQ-9 ADOLESCENT = 16/27 MODERATELY SEVERE depression with positive ITEM 9 (thoughts of being better off dead or hurting yourself score 1 'several days'); GAD-7 = 12/21 moderate anxiety; ASQ positive (#1 YES, #2-4 NO) — full risk assessment with C-SSRS confirms passive ideation without plan/intent/access/prior attempts; CRAFFT 0/6 + SCOFF 0/5 (rule out high-risk substance + eating disorder)
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — depression + anxiety with passive suicidal ideation. Validated screening tools quantify severity. POSITIVE PHQ-9 item 9 requires follow-up regardless of total score. C-SSRS + ASQ confirm risk stratification = MODERATE risk. Standard quantitative + qualitative assessment guides intervention level.

opts.text: RISK FACTORS for adolescent depression + suicide: 6-mo weight loss 3 kg (clinically significant), academic drop A → B−/C+ this semester, sleep onset insomnia × 6 wk (~5.5 hr/night vs 8–10 recommended), recent move 4 mo ago (loss of friends), bullying at new school, boyfriend breakup 5 wk ago, stopped enjoying art + reading (anhedonia), 5+ hr/day social media with negative impact, family conflict with stepfather, FIREARMS in home + access to mother's medications in unlocked drawer
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — risk factor constellation. Multiple modifiable risk factors. ANHEDONIA + somatic symptoms + sleep + appetite + academic + social isolation = depression syndrome. LETHAL MEANS access (firearms + medications) is a critical risk factor for completed suicide — means restriction is most effective single intervention.

opts.text: PROTECTIVE FACTORS: supportive maternal grandmother ('my person'), 9-year-old half-brother (close + protective), family dog, soccer team participation, identifies as cisgender female + heterosexual (low identity-related minority stress in this context), no prior suicide attempts, no self-harm behaviors, no current substance use, no active plan/intent/access ongoing (means restriction in progress), receptive to therapy + MH intake, family engaged + cooperative, insurance coverage, PCP medical home with continuity, Buddhist spiritual heritage, brief symptom duration (~2–3 months)
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — protective factors guide risk stratification + intervention level. Multiple protective factors balance risk factors. Family engagement + same-day MH intake + means restriction + outpatient therapy = MODERATE risk appropriate for outpatient management. Strengths-based assessment essential for adolescent care.

opts.text: CONFIDENTIALITY framework: established with patient + caregiver at visit start; minor consent rights (CA Family Code §§6924-6929 mental health + reproductive + STI + substance use at 12+); limits = mandated reporting (child abuse/neglect) + IMMINENT DANGER (active SI with plan/intent/access) + court orders; CONFIDENTIAL HEADSSS portion with mother out of room ~20 min; documentation in sensitive section of EHR separated from parent portal per minor consent + privacy
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — confidentiality is foundational to adolescent care + builds therapeutic alliance + supports honest disclosure. Minor consent laws + exceptions (mandated reporting + imminent danger). CONFIDENTIAL portion essential + structured. Documentation per institutional minor-consent privacy protocol.

opts.text: SAFETY PLAN (Stanley-Brown brief intervention) + LETHAL MEANS RESTRICTION: 6-step plan with warning signs + internal coping + social distractions + people for support + professionals + means restriction (firearms relocated to off-site safe + medications secured); SAME-DAY MH intake (LCSW within 3 hr of PCP referral); CBT outpatient therapy weekly × initial 8 sessions; antidepressant deferred pending therapy response; 988 + 741741 + ER crisis resources
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — safety planning + means restriction are evidence-based suicide prevention interventions. STANLEY-BROWN is brief evidence-based. LETHAL MEANS RESTRICTION (firearms relocated out of home most protective) is most effective single intervention. SAME-DAY MH intake addresses moderate risk in outpatient setting. CRISIS resources available.

opts.text: ACIP IMMUNIZATIONS reviewed + administered today: HPV complete (2-dose initiated <15 yr); Tdap complete (age 11); MenACWY complete age 11 + BOOSTER due age 16 (~1 yr); MenB SCDM at age 16 visit; INFLUENZA quadrivalent 0.5 mL IM deltoid R administered + VIS + 15-min observation; COVID-19 up to date; HBV/HAV/MMR/VAR complete childhood
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — ACIP immunizations are Health Promotion core. HPV is HIGH-VALUE (cancer prevention). MenACWY booster due age 16. Influenza annually. CA AB 499 allows minor consent for STI-related vaccines (including HPV) at 12+. RN role: review + administer + counsel + VIS + observation.

opts.text: SPORTS PHYSICAL CLEARANCE per PPE-5: cardiac + MSK + concussion + female athlete triad/RED-S history + exam unremarkable for SCA risk; CLEARANCE HELD pending mental health stability + LCSW clearance + nutrition + sleep improvement; continued gentle soccer training appropriate if MH-team-approved (for emotional + social + structural benefit); formal clearance form pending
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — sports clearance balances participation benefits + safety. PPE-5 standard. SCA risk + female athlete triad considered. Mental health stability before clearance. CONTINUED gentle training appropriate per MH team — sports can be therapeutic + structuring.

opts.text: ANTICIPATORY GUIDANCE + LIFESTYLE INTERVENTIONS: sleep hygiene (9 hr nightly); nutrition (3 meals + snacks + family meal patterns + favorite foods reintroduced + calcium + iron + vitamin D); physical activity (60 min/day moderate-vigorous); screen time limits + media literacy + healthy tech relationships; healthy relationships + consent education; substance use harm reduction; safety (seatbelts + helmets + driving + alcohol/drug-free + means restriction); identity + autonomy + future planning
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — comprehensive Health Promotion anticipatory guidance. Tailored to adolescent developmental stage + risk factors + strengths. Bright Futures recommendations. Lifestyle interventions adjunctive to MH treatment.

opts.text: PARENT ENGAGEMENT with PATIENT CONSENT for safety info (age-appropriate boundary): share safety concerns + plan + means restriction + same-day MH intake + crisis resources + general depression/anxiety symptoms; DO NOT share full HEADSSS details (boyfriend + bullying + family conflict + identity nuances) per patient consent; family engaged + cooperative + grateful; family meeting with MH intake 1700
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — confidentiality-parent balance navigated thoughtfully. PARENT engaged as partner with patient consent for safety info. AGE-APPROPRIATE boundary preserves trust + therapeutic alliance + privacy. FAMILY supports outcome. RN role: facilitate balanced disclosure + document.

opts.text: Allergies: NKDA documented
opts.rat: Reassuring — no documented allergies. Document and proceed. No medication allergies in immunization or treatment regimen selection. Not the priority cue at this visit but important for safe care.

opts.text: Inform mother of ALL details of the HEADSSS interview (including boyfriend breakup + bullying + family conflict + identity nuances) without patient consent because mother is the legal guardian and adolescents do not have privacy rights from parents
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. Adolescents have CONFIDENTIALITY rights per minor consent laws (CA Family Code §§6924-6929) for mental health + reproductive + STI + substance use services from age 12+. Sharing without patient consent VIOLATES confidentiality + damages therapeutic alliance + may breach minor consent privacy. CORRECT: share safety info with patient consent for partial disclosure; full HEADSSS details remain private unless patient consents OR imminent danger/mandated reporting threshold is met. AGE-APPROPRIATE boundary respects autonomy + supports honest disclosure in future.

opts.text: Refer to ED immediately for psychiatric hospitalization given any positive PHQ-9 + any positive ASQ — outpatient management is not safe for adolescent with passive suicidal ideation
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach. SUICIDE RISK STRATIFICATION guides intervention level. THIS PATIENT = passive ideation + no plan/intent/access ongoing/prior attempts + protective factors + family engagement + same-day MH intake + safety plan + means restriction = MODERATE risk appropriate for OUTPATIENT MANAGEMENT. ED referral + psychiatric hospitalization reserved for: active ideation with plan/intent, active access to lethal means without removal, recent attempts, inability to maintain safety in outpatient setting, severe symptoms. Over-hospitalization can be traumatic + counterproductive + miss the opportunity for therapeutic alliance + family engagement + means restriction at home.

steps.title: Analyze Cues
steps.sub: What adolescent health + suicide prevention + Health Promotion principles apply?
steps.inst: Select ALL accurate linkages between cues and underlying principles.

opts.text: ADOLESCENT WELL-CARE structure per Bright Futures + AAP: joint history with caregiver → CONFIDENTIAL HEADSSS interview alone (~20 min) → joint summary + plan with patient consent; CONFIDENTIALITY framed first with limits (mandated reporting + imminent danger + court orders); MINOR CONSENT in CA (Family Code §§6924-6929) for mental health + reproductive + STI + substance use at 12+; institutional EHR separates sensitive content from parent portal per minor consent + privacy
opts.rat: CORRECT — adolescent well-care framework. CONFIDENTIAL portion central to therapeutic alliance + honest disclosure. Minor consent rights protect access. Exceptions enable safety. Documentation per institutional privacy protocol.

opts.text: HEADSSS / HEEADSSS / SSHADESS confidential psychosocial interview: H=Home + E=Education + A=Activities + D=Drugs + S=Sexuality + S=Suicide/MH + S=Safety; expanded adds Eating disorders + Spirituality + Strengths; PRINCIPLES = rapport + low-stakes → high-stakes + open-ended + non-judgmental + DIRECT + structured screening tools (PHQ-9, GAD-7, CRAFFT, SCOFF, C-SSRS, ASQ) + respect pace + strengths-based
opts.rat: CORRECT — HEADSSS framework. Structured psychosocial assessment. Direct + non-judgmental + structured screening tools. Strengths + resilience addressed. RN role: facilitate + administer + recognize.

opts.text: PHQ-9 cutoffs: 5–9 mild, 10–14 moderate, 15–19 moderately severe, ≥20 severe; ITEM 9 (self-harm thoughts) always requires follow-up regardless of total; C-SSRS stratifies suicide risk (passive ideation → active ideation with method/intent/plan + behavior); ASQ rapid 4-item screen positive if ANY yes → full risk assessment; risk levels guide intervention (safety plan + outpatient vs ED + inpatient)
opts.rat: CORRECT — validated screening tools quantify + standardize. POSITIVE PHQ-9 item 9 always requires follow-up. C-SSRS + ASQ stratify risk. Risk levels guide intervention. RN role: administer + document + follow up + escalate.

opts.text: SUICIDE RISK STRATIFICATION = MODERATE for this patient: passive ideation + no plan/intent/access ongoing/prior attempts + multiple protective factors + family engagement + same-day MH intake + safety plan + means restriction = OUTPATIENT MANAGEMENT appropriate; HIGHER risk (active ideation with plan/intent, prior attempts, severe symptoms, inability to maintain safety) may require ED + psychiatric hospitalization
opts.rat: CORRECT — risk stratification framework. Moderate risk + protective factors + same-day MH intake + means restriction + safety plan = appropriate outpatient management. ED + inpatient reserved for higher risk + active threat + inability to maintain safety. Over-hospitalization counterproductive.

opts.text: STANLEY-BROWN SAFETY PLAN = 6 steps personalized: warning signs + internal coping + social contacts for distraction + people for support + professionals + crisis + MEANS RESTRICTION; LETHAL MEANS RESTRICTION = MOST EFFECTIVE single intervention (firearms relocated out of home most protective; medications secured; sharp objects + ropes secured); CALM counseling parents
opts.rat: CORRECT — Stanley-Brown safety plan + lethal means restriction. Evidence-based brief intervention. Means restriction central — firearms relocated out of home most protective. CALM counseling. Documented + shared with family. Updated as needed.

opts.text: ADOLESCENT DEPRESSION TREATMENT — stepped care: mild = supportive + monitor; moderate = CBT or IPT-A weekly × 8–16 sessions; moderately severe or severe = combined CBT + SSRI; FLUOXETINE FDA-approved for adolescent MDD ≥8 yr (only fully approved SSRI); escitalopram approved 12+; sertraline OCD only; AVOID paroxetine; BLACK-BOX warning for SI in <25 yr requires informed consent + frequent monitoring (weekly × 4 → biweekly × 4 → monthly) + crisis plan
opts.rat: CORRECT — adolescent depression treatment framework. Stepped care. CBT first-line for moderate. Combined CBT + SSRI for moderately severe + severe. Fluoxetine most evidence-based. Black-box warning + monitoring.

opts.text: ACIP IMMUNIZATIONS for adolescents: Tdap age 11–12; HPV 2-dose if <15 yr OR 3-dose if ≥15 yr at initiation; MenACWY age 11–12 + BOOSTER age 16; MenB SCDM age 16–23; influenza annually; COVID per current guidance; HBV/HAV/MMR/VAR catch-up; CA AB 499 allows minor consent for STI-related vaccines (including HPV) at 12+; HPV is HIGH-VALUE cancer prevention
opts.rat: CORRECT — ACIP immunizations framework. HPV high-value. MenACWY booster at 16. SCDM for MenB. CA minor consent for STI-related vaccines. RN role: review + administer + counsel + VIS + observation.

opts.text: SPORTS PHYSICAL CLEARANCE per PPE-5: comprehensive history + exam + SCA risk + female athlete triad/RED-S + concussion + mental health + ergogenic substances; CLEARED, CLEARED with recommendations, CLEARED after evaluation, NOT CLEARED outcomes; SCA leading causes = HCM + coronary anomalies + long-QT + myocarditis + commotio cordis; EAP + AED at every facility; concussion management = REMOVE + evaluate + GRADUAL RTP
opts.rat: CORRECT — PPE-5 sports clearance framework. SCA risk + female athlete triad + concussion + mental health. EAP + AED. CONCUSSION management. Clearance balances participation benefits + safety.

opts.text: Confidentiality of HEADSSS interview is ABSOLUTE — RN should never share any of the content with mother under any circumstance, even if patient discloses imminent suicide plan with intent + means
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. CONFIDENTIALITY has LIMITS clearly explained at outset: (1) mandated reporting (child abuse/neglect), (2) IMMINENT DANGER to self or others (active SI with plan/intent/access OR homicide ideation), (3) court orders. IMMINENT DANGER (suicide plan + intent + means) IS A CONFIDENTIALITY EXCEPTION + RN/PCP can + should share with caregiver + activate emergency services. Adolescent's confidentiality is supported BUT NOT at the cost of their safety. Communicating limits at outset + honoring exceptions when triggered.

opts.text: All adolescents who score positive on PHQ-9 item 9 OR ASQ should be admitted to a psychiatric facility for evaluation
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. RISK STRATIFICATION guides intervention. PASSIVE ideation + no plan/intent/access + protective factors + family engagement + outpatient MH intake + safety plan + means restriction = OUTPATIENT management appropriate (this patient's situation). ACTIVE ideation with plan/intent/access OR prior attempts OR inability to maintain safety OR severe symptoms = ED + possible inpatient. Over-hospitalization can be traumatic + counterproductive + miss therapeutic alliance + family engagement opportunity. Use validated risk stratification not blanket admission.

opts.text: Black-box warning means SSRIs are CONTRAINDICATED in adolescents under 25 — they should NEVER be prescribed because they cause suicide
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG interpretation. BLACK-BOX WARNING indicates INCREASED RISK of SI in first weeks of treatment in patients <25 yr; the warning requires informed consent + family education + frequent follow-up + crisis plan — NOT contraindication. UNTREATED severe depression has HIGHER suicide risk than treated depression with appropriate SSRI + monitoring. FLUOXETINE is FDA-APPROVED for adolescent MDD ≥8 yr (only fully approved SSRI for pediatric depression) precisely because benefits outweigh risks for moderately severe + severe cases. Stepped care approach: CBT first-line for moderate; combined CBT + SSRI for moderately severe + severe.

steps.title: Prioritize Hypotheses
steps.sub: Rank nursing concerns at this adolescent visit
steps.inst: Rank these nursing concerns from #1 (HIGHEST priority) to #5 (LOWEST).

opts.text: Suicide Risk Assessment + Safety Planning + Lethal Means Restriction r/t passive suicidal ideation in moderately severe depression; Stanley-Brown safety plan; FIREARMS relocated to off-site safe today; medications secured; same-day LCSW intake at 1700; crisis resources (988 + 741741 + ER); follow-up MH + PCP
opts.rat: #1 — HIGHEST. Safety is the immediate priority. Passive SI + access to firearms + medications = lethal means + risk. Means restriction is most effective single intervention. Safety plan + same-day MH intake address current risk. Foundation for all subsequent care.

opts.text: Confidentiality + Parent Engagement Balance r/t age-appropriate disclosure with patient consent — share safety concerns + plan + means restriction WITHOUT disclosing full HEADSSS details (boyfriend + bullying + family conflict + identity nuances); minor consent rights (CA FC §§6924-6929 mental health/reproductive/STI/substance 12+); confidentiality exceptions (mandated reporting + imminent danger) clearly communicated
opts.rat: #2 — HIGH. Confidentiality is foundational to adolescent therapeutic alliance + honest disclosure. PARENT engagement as partner with patient consent enables safety while preserving trust. Minor consent rights respected. AGE-APPROPRIATE boundary supports both safety + autonomy. RN navigates with nuance.

opts.text: Mental Health Treatment Initiation + Follow-Up Coordination r/t moderate depression + anxiety; same-day LCSW intake; outpatient CBT weekly × 8 sessions; antidepressant deferred pending therapy response; PCP follow-up 2 weeks + monthly; school counselor coordination via release of information; family supports + grandmother + crisis resources; consideration of fluoxetine if therapy response inadequate at 6–8 weeks
opts.rat: #3 — HIGH. Treatment initiation + coordination establish therapeutic foundation. Stepped care (CBT first for moderate). Same-day intake + outpatient follow-up + crisis resources + school coordination. Family engagement essential.

opts.text: Health Promotion + ACIP Immunizations + Anticipatory Guidance r/t Bright Futures adolescent well-care; influenza administered + VIS + observation; HPV/Tdap/MenACWY status reviewed + booster planning at 16; SCDM for MenB at 16; sleep hygiene + nutrition + physical activity + screen time + healthy relationships + consent education + safety (seatbelts + helmets + driving + means restriction) + identity + autonomy + future planning
opts.rat: #4 — Significant + ongoing. Health Promotion core function of adolescent well-care. Immunizations + anticipatory guidance + lifestyle interventions adjunctive to MH treatment. Bright Futures comprehensive.

opts.text: Sports Physical Clearance + Continued Activity r/t PPE-5 evaluation; cardiac + MSK + concussion + female athlete triad/RED-S history + exam unremarkable; CLEARANCE HELD pending mental health stability + LCSW clearance + nutrition + sleep improvement; continued gentle soccer training appropriate if MH-team-approved (emotional + social + structural benefit)
opts.rat: #5 — Important + ongoing. Sports clearance balances participation benefits + safety. PPE-5. Mental health stability before clearance. Continued gentle training appropriate per MH team — sports can be therapeutic + structuring.

steps.title: Generate Solutions
steps.inst: For EACH intervention, classify as INDICATED or NOT INDICATED for this adolescent at this visit.

opts.text: ADOLESCENT WELL-CARE structure: joint with mother 10 min → CONFIDENTIAL HEADSSS alone ~20 min → joint summary + plan with patient consent; CONFIDENTIALITY framed first with limits (mandated reporting + imminent danger + court orders); MINOR CONSENT per CA FC §§6924-6929 mental health + reproductive + STI + substance use at 12+; sensitive content separated from parent portal
opts.rat: Foundational structure for adolescent well-care. Confidential portion essential. Minor consent rights respected. Documentation per institutional privacy protocol.

opts.text: Administer screening tools: PHQ-9 adolescent + GAD-7 + C-SSRS + ASQ + CRAFFT + SCOFF + sexuality + safety screens; QUANTIFY + standardize; positive PHQ-9 item 9 + positive ASQ trigger full risk assessment; risk stratification guides intervention
opts.rat: Validated screening tools quantify + standardize + guide intervention. Multiple domains screened. POSITIVE ITEM 9 or ASQ triggers full risk assessment. RN role: administer + document + follow up.

opts.text: STANLEY-BROWN SAFETY PLAN + LETHAL MEANS RESTRICTION: 6-step plan personalized; FIREARMS relocated to off-site safe (stepfather's brother) × 30+ days; MEDICATIONS secured (mother's antidepressants relocated to bedroom locked cabinet); SAME-DAY LCSW intake; outpatient CBT therapy; 988 + 741741 + ER crisis resources; written copies + family review
opts.rat: Evidence-based suicide prevention. Means restriction most effective single intervention. Safety plan brief + structured. Same-day MH intake addresses moderate risk. Crisis resources available. Family engaged.

opts.text: ACIP IMMUNIZATIONS administered + reviewed: HPV complete (2-dose <15 yr); Tdap complete; MenACWY booster due age 16 (~1 yr); MenB SCDM at 16; INFLUENZA quadrivalent 0.5 mL IM deltoid R administered today + VIS provided + 15-min observation; COVID up to date; HBV/HAV/MMR/VAR childhood complete
opts.rat: ACIP recommendations + Bright Futures + Health Promotion core. Influenza annually. HPV high-value. MenACWY booster planning. RN role: review + administer + VIS + observation.

opts.text: PARENT ENGAGEMENT WITH PATIENT CONSENT for safety info — share safety concerns + plan + means restriction + same-day MH intake + crisis resources + general depression/anxiety symptoms; DO NOT share full HEADSSS details (boyfriend + bullying + family conflict + identity nuances) per patient consent; family meeting 1700 with MH intake; written crisis resources
opts.rat: Confidentiality-parent balance navigated thoughtfully with patient consent. AGE-APPROPRIATE boundary respected. PARENT engaged as partner. Family meeting + crisis resources support safety + therapeutic alliance.

opts.text: LIFESTYLE INTERVENTIONS: sleep hygiene (9 hr nightly); nutrition (3 meals + snacks + family meal patterns + favorite foods reintroduced); physical activity (60 min/day moderate-vigorous + continue gentle soccer if MH-team-approved); screen time limits + media literacy; healthy relationships + consent education; substance use harm reduction; safety (seatbelts + helmets + driving + alcohol/drug-free + means restriction); identity + autonomy + future planning
opts.rat: Comprehensive Bright Futures anticipatory guidance. Tailored to adolescent + risk factors + strengths. Adjunctive to MH treatment. Multiple Health Promotion domains addressed.

opts.text: Refer to ED immediately for psychiatric hospitalization given any positive PHQ-9 + any positive ASQ — outpatient management is not safe for adolescent with passive suicidal ideation
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach. RISK STRATIFICATION = MODERATE (passive ideation + no plan/intent/access + protective factors + family engagement + same-day MH intake + safety plan + means restriction). OUTPATIENT management appropriate for moderate risk. ED + inpatient reserved for active threat + inability to maintain safety. Over-hospitalization can be traumatic + counterproductive.

opts.text: Disclose to mother ALL details of the HEADSSS interview (boyfriend breakup + bullying + family conflict + identity nuances) without patient consent because mother is the legal guardian and adolescents do not have privacy rights from parents
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. Adolescents have CONFIDENTIALITY rights per minor consent laws (CA FC §§6924-6929). Sharing without patient consent violates confidentiality + damages therapeutic alliance + may breach minor consent privacy. CORRECT: share safety info with patient consent for partial disclosure; full HEADSSS details remain private unless patient consents OR imminent danger/mandated reporting threshold met. AGE-APPROPRIATE boundary respects autonomy + supports future honest disclosure.

opts.text: Start FLUOXETINE 10 mg PO daily today + administer first dose in clinic before discharge; the patient has moderately severe depression and SSRI is first-line per FDA approval
opts.rat: ⚠️ NOT APPROPRIATE today. STEPPED CARE = CBT first-line for moderate (PHQ-9 10–14 moderate, 15–19 moderately severe — this patient 16 is at boundary); CBT can be initiated first with antidepressant deferred pending therapy response at 6–8 weeks. BLACK-BOX warning requires informed consent + family education + crisis plan + frequent monitoring (weekly × 4 → biweekly × 4 → monthly). Starting SSRI same-day without MH evaluation + family preference + informed consent is premature. APPROPRIATE NEXT STEP: outpatient LCSW intake → CBT initiation → re-evaluate at 6–8 weeks → consider SSRI if inadequate response.

opts.text: Issue formal sports clearance today for soccer participation since cardiac + MSK + concussion screening are unremarkable and physical exam is normal
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach. SPORTS CLEARANCE per PPE-5 considers MENTAL HEALTH + NUTRITION + female athlete triad/RED-S in addition to cardiac + MSK + concussion. THIS PATIENT has moderately severe depression + recent weight loss + sleep disturbance + active MH evaluation in progress — CLEARANCE HELD pending mental health stability + LCSW clearance + nutrition + sleep improvement is appropriate. Continued gentle soccer training appropriate if MH-team-approved (for emotional + social + structural benefit). Formal clearance form pending.

opts.text: Pressure the patient to disclose her boyfriend breakup + bullying details to her mother + tell her she must choose her relationship with her mom over her privacy; threaten that her mother will be told regardless if she doesn't agree
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach + multiple ethical violations. (1) PRESSURE/COERCION violates adolescent autonomy + trauma-informed care + may revictimize patient; (2) confidentiality is the foundation of therapeutic alliance + threats damage trust + future honest disclosure; (3) family conflict + boyfriend dynamics are content for therapy + may emerge over time as patient comfort develops; (4) AGE-APPROPRIATE boundary = safety info shared (consent) + full HEADSSS content remains private (consent); (5) imminent danger threshold not crossed = no override authority. CORRECT: respect patient consent + share what they agree to share + build trust + therapy explores deeper content in safe setting.

opts.text: Coordinate same-day mental health intake at 1700 with LCSW at same medical center; outpatient CBT weekly × initial 8 sessions; PCP follow-up 2 weeks + monthly until stable; school counselor coordination via signed release of information; family supports + grandmother encouraged; crisis resources 988 + 741741 + ED if active SI/plan/intent emerges
opts.rat: Standard appropriate intervention for moderate-risk adolescent depression. SAME-DAY MH intake addresses risk. CBT first-line for moderate. PCP follow-up + monitoring. School coordination. Family supports. Crisis resources. Comprehensive coordination.

steps.title: Take Action
steps.sub: FIRST action sequence at this adolescent visit
steps.inst: Rank the order in which the RN should implement these actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: ESTABLISH CONFIDENTIALITY framework + minor consent rights with patient + caregiver at visit start (limits = mandated reporting + imminent danger + court orders); separate caregiver from patient for CONFIDENTIAL HEADSSS interview (~20 min); administer validated screening tools (PHQ-9 + GAD-7 + C-SSRS + ASQ + CRAFFT + SCOFF) during confidential portion
opts.rat: #1 — FIRST. Foundation of adolescent care. CONFIDENTIALITY enables honest disclosure + risk identification. Without this framework, screening yields are lower + risk missed. RN role: facilitate visit format + frame confidentiality + administer tools.

opts.text: CONDUCT suicide risk stratification using C-SSRS + ASQ + clinical assessment: passive ideation YES + active ideation with method/intent/plan NO + no prior attempts + protective factors present + family engagement = MODERATE RISK appropriate for outpatient management; document + escalate to PCP
opts.rat: #2 — Second. Risk stratification guides intervention level. Quantitative + qualitative assessment. Determines outpatient vs ED + inpatient pathway. RN role: administer + document + escalate.

opts.text: CO-CREATE Stanley-Brown SAFETY PLAN with 6 steps personalized (warning signs + internal coping + social distractions + people for support + professionals + crisis + LETHAL MEANS RESTRICTION); arrange SAME-DAY LCSW intake at 1700; activate crisis resources 988 + 741741 + ER; coordinate firearm relocation + medication securing today
opts.rat: #3 — Third. Safety planning + means restriction are time-sensitive evidence-based interventions. Same-day MH intake essential for moderate risk. Crisis resources available. Family engaged with means restriction. Foundation for outpatient management.

opts.text: JOINT PORTION with mother (with patient consent) — share safety concerns + plan + means restriction + same-day MH intake + crisis resources + general depression/anxiety symptoms; DO NOT share full HEADSSS details per patient consent (age-appropriate boundary); family meeting at MH intake 1700; written crisis resources + lifestyle handouts
opts.rat: #4 — Fourth. Family engagement with patient consent supports safety + therapeutic alliance. Age-appropriate disclosure. Family meeting + written resources reinforce. Mother engaged + cooperative + grateful.

opts.text: ACIP IMMUNIZATIONS + LIFESTYLE INTERVENTIONS + ANTICIPATORY GUIDANCE + SPORTS CLEARANCE PROCESSING: administer influenza + review HPV/Tdap/MenACWY status + booster planning at 16 + SCDM MenB; sleep hygiene + nutrition + physical activity + screen time + healthy relationships + consent education + safety + identity + future planning; sports clearance HELD pending MH stability + LCSW clearance
opts.rat: #5 — Ongoing. Health Promotion core + anticipatory guidance + immunizations + sports clearance coordination. Bright Futures comprehensive. Foundation for healthy adolescent development.

steps.title: Evaluate Outcomes
steps.inst: For each finding, classify the trajectory.

opts.text: End of well-care visit Day 0 1500: SAFETY PLAN co-created + LCSW intake at 1700 confirmed + firearms relocation in progress today + medications secured today + crisis resources provided + family meeting scheduled + ACIP influenza administered + comprehensive anticipatory guidance provided + sports clearance HELD with continued gentle training pending MH clearance + outpatient therapy + PCP follow-up arranged + family engaged + cooperative + grateful
opts.rat: Excellent visit outcome — comprehensive risk assessment + safety planning + same-day MH intake + means restriction + family engagement + Health Promotion + immunizations + anticipatory guidance + sports clearance coordination + follow-up planning. Standard expected best-case adolescent well-care + MH intervention outcome.

opts.text: 1700 same-day LCSW intake: confirmed PCP findings + completed Stanley-Brown safety plan in conjunction with patient + mother; CBT-based outpatient therapy initiated weekly × 8 sessions; antidepressant deferred pending therapy response; firearm relocation completed (stepfather took guns to brother's safe today); medications secured (mother's antidepressants in locked bedroom cabinet); patient verbalizes understanding + receptive + grandmother phone call scheduled; family meeting positive; follow-up LCSW 1 week + PCP 2 weeks + crisis lines 24/7
opts.rat: Excellent same-day MH intake outcome — safety plan + lethal means restriction completed + therapy initiated + family engaged + receptive patient + follow-up arranged. This is the value of same-day intervention + multidisciplinary care. Standard expected trajectory with appropriate management.

opts.text: 2-week PCP follow-up Day 14: PHQ-9 12/27 (improved from 16 — moderate now); GAD-7 8/21 (improved); ASQ #1 NO + #2-4 NO (improving); sleep improved 7.5 hr/night; weight stable + appetite improving + 2 meals daily; academic engaged + return to art class twice this week; soccer try-outs in 2 weeks with conditional clearance pending continued progress + LCSW clearance; CBT 2 sessions completed + receptive; family supports engaged + grandmother visits weekly; means restriction maintained; no SI/plan/intent
opts.rat: Standard expected 2-week trajectory — symptom improvement + functional improvement + engagement + adherence + family support. Standard response to CBT + lifestyle interventions + safety plan + means restriction. Conditional sports clearance approaching. Pre-medication trial sufficient for moderate cases.

opts.text: 6-week PCP follow-up: PHQ-9 8/27 (mild); GAD-7 6/21 (mild); ASQ all NO; sleep 8 hr; weight gained 1 kg; academic A-/B+; art class active + completed first sculpture + sharing on grandmother's birthday; soccer try-outs successful + made varsity + receiving from coach + emotional benefit; CBT 6 sessions + skills developing; means restriction maintained; receptive to ongoing therapy + monthly PCP; family supports continued
opts.rat: Excellent 6-week trajectory — substantial symptom + functional improvement + integration of activities + soccer success + family/grandmother + ongoing therapy. CBT response evident. Antidepressant not started + not needed at this trajectory. Standard expected positive outcome for moderate adolescent depression with appropriate intervention.

opts.text: Day 14 — patient has acute worsening: stopped attending CBT (missed 2 sessions); social withdrawal increased; statements 'maybe everyone really would be better off'; PHQ-9 22/27 (severe); ASQ #1 YES + #2 YES (new) + #3 NO + #4 NO; family contacts PCP urgently; ED evaluation arranged + psychiatric hospitalization considered + LCSW activated + reassessment of suicide risk + escalation to ACTIVE ideation territory
opts.rat: ⚠️ ESCALATION — worsening trajectory requires immediate response. Stopped therapy + social withdrawal + worsening PHQ-9 + new ASQ #2 yes = increased risk. ED evaluation + reassessment + psychiatric consultation appropriate. Re-evaluate level of care + safety plan + means restriction + potential medication initiation + family engagement intensification. RN role: recognize early + escalate.

opts.text: Day 7 — mother calls saying 'I'm worried she's not really doing better; she's still in her room a lot and not eating much'; mother + patient seen for unscheduled visit: PHQ-9 14/27 (slight improvement but moderate); GAD-7 10/21 (slight improvement); ASQ unchanged (#1 sometimes YES + others NO); patient reports therapy 'is starting to help but it's hard'; sleep mildly improved 6.5 hr/night; safety plan reviewed + means restriction maintained; encouragement + adjustments to lifestyle plan + family meeting 1 week later; continued therapy + follow-up
opts.rat: Standard expected slow progress in adolescent depression treatment. CBT takes 4–8 weeks for full response. Early symptoms can be slow to change. Family-PCP communication appropriate. Safety plan + means restriction reinforced. Patience + continued care + adjustments. NOT improving rapidly + NOT worsening — this is expected unchanged-but-progressing pattern.

opts.text: Day 30 — family meeting + LCSW + PCP review: substantial improvement maintained; PHQ-9 trending 16 → 12 → 8 over 4 weeks; GAD-7 trending 12 → 10 → 6; ASQ all NO; weight gained 1.5 kg; sleep 8 hr; academic B+ → A−; soccer training continued + cleared after 4 weeks of stability + grandmother + family + therapy + means restriction maintained; relationship with stepfather improved with family therapy session; antidepressant continued to be deferred per response; ongoing weekly CBT + monthly PCP
opts.rat: Excellent 30-day trajectory. Standard expected positive outcome with appropriate intervention. Demonstrates value of stepped care (CBT for moderate, antidepressant deferred until response inadequate). Family + grandmother + means restriction + sports/identity all supportive. Standard ideal adolescent depression treatment outcome.

opts.text: Day 21 — RN identifies during chart audit that patient + mother were not adequately advised about FIREARM means restriction; firearms still in home (in safe but accessible to patient if she learned combination); RN escalates to PCP + social work + arranges urgent family meeting + reinforces means restriction recommendations + connects with CALM Counseling on Access to Lethal Means program + offers temporary off-site storage logistics; firearms relocated within 48 hours
opts.rat: ⚠️ MEANS RESTRICTION GAP identified — protective factor was missed/incomplete at initial visit. RN advocacy + escalation appropriate response. Firearms in same-house safe is INADEQUATE means restriction in active SI context — TEMPORARY OFF-SITE storage is more protective. Connecting with CALM program + family + arranging relocation within 48 hr appropriate. Just culture + system improvement + RN advocacy. The identified gap is worsening but the response is corrective + appropriate.

opts.text: 1-year follow-up: 16 y/o now; PHQ-9 4/27 (subclinical); GAD-7 3/21 (subclinical); ASQ all NO × 6 months; weight gained 5 kg (BMI 21 healthy); academic A; soccer varsity continuing; art portfolio for college applications; grandmother + family supportive; stepfather relationship improved; CBT completed at 6 months + maintenance check-ins; antidepressant never required; means restriction relaxed gradually with continued family safety culture; MenB SCDM completed + MenACWY booster administered + flu + COVID + HPV booster (catch-up for 9-valent expanded indication ages 27–45); driving license + drivers ed + safe driving + GDL + future planning + college visits
opts.rat: Excellent 1-year outcome — sustained remission + functional improvement + family + identity + future + immunization compliance + safety culture maintained. Demonstrates the value of comprehensive multidisciplinary + family-engaged + capacity-sensitive adolescent care + appropriate stepped care. Standard ideal long-term outcome.

opts.text: Day 5 emergency — patient sent text to friend 'Maybe I should just end it'; friend told her own mother who called Mrs. Tanaka who called 988 + brought patient to ED; ED evaluation = active SI with method (her stepfather's guns — which she found combination for) + intent (within hours) + plan; emergent psychiatric admission; firearms removed by stepfather to brother's safe + lock combination changed; medications removed from home; lessons learned from initial means restriction adequacy review; medication initiation with fluoxetine 10 mg in hospital with informed consent + family education + safety monitoring
opts.rat: ⚠️ EMERGENT — active SI with plan + intent + means access = ACUTE risk → ED + psychiatric hospitalization indicated. Critical escalation. Initial means restriction was inadequate (gun safe in home with combination not securely held + patient learned combination). Family intervention + 988 + ED appropriate emergency response. Lessons: temporary off-site storage more protective than in-home safe. Fluoxetine initiation appropriate at this severity level with black-box warning informed consent + monitoring.

title: Severe Preeclampsia with HELLP Syndrome in 32-Year-Old G1P0 at 34 Weeks + 2 Days: Magnesium Sulfate Seizure Prophylaxis with DTR + Respiratory + UOP Toxicity Surveillance + Calcium Gluconate Antidote, Severe-Range Blood Pressure Treatment within 60 Minutes, Antenatal Betamethasone for Fetal Lung Maturity, Delivery Planning, and Postpartum Eclampsia Vigilance × 24–48 Hours
subtitle: SEVERE PREECLAMPSIA diagnostic criteria per ACOG 2020 = preeclampsia (new HTN ≥140/90 + proteinuria OR end-organ dysfunction at ≥20 wk in previously normotensive woman) PLUS ≥1 severe feature (BP ≥160/110 sustained × ≥4 hr or treated; PLATELETS <100k; AST/ALT ≥2× ULN; new HEPATIC pain unresponsive to medication; SERUM Cr ≥1.1 or doubled; PULMONARY EDEMA; new visual or cerebral symptoms); HELLP syndrome = HEMOLYSIS (schistocytes + LDH ≥600 OR T bili ≥1.2 OR haptoglobin <30) + ELEVATED LIVER enzymes (AST or ALT ≥2× ULN; often >70 IU/L) + LOW PLATELETS (<100k); MISSISSIPPI classification (class 1 platelets ≤50k = worst; class 2 50–100k; class 3 100–150k); TENNESSEE classification (complete vs incomplete); MAGNESIUM SULFATE 4–6 g IV loading dose over 15–20 min then 1–2 g/hr maintenance infusion for SEIZURE PROPHYLAXIS + neuroprotection — TOXICITY MONITORING q1h: DEEP TENDON REFLEXES (loss = early toxicity at Mg 10), RESPIRATORY RATE (<12/min = severe toxicity at Mg 12–14), URINE OUTPUT (<30 mL/hr = renal accumulation risk; magnesium renally cleared), MENTAL STATUS, magnesium levels (therapeutic 4–7 mEq/L; loss of patellar reflex at 8–10; respiratory depression 10–13; cardiac arrest >25); CALCIUM GLUCONATE 1 g (10 mL of 10%) IV SLOW over 3 minutes is the ANTIDOTE for magnesium toxicity — keep available at bedside; HOLD magnesium for ANY toxicity sign + administer calcium gluconate + monitor + reassess + restart at lower rate if appropriate; SEVERE-RANGE BP (≥160/110 sustained) treated WITHIN 60 MINUTES per ACOG to prevent stroke + abruption; IV LABETALOL 20 mg → 40 → 80 mg q10 min (max cumulative 220 mg) OR IV HYDRALAZINE 5–10 mg q20 min OR PO NIFEDIPINE 10 mg q20 min (no longer requires order to delay nifedipine if IV access not available); AVOID ACE-I/ARB in pregnancy (teratogenic); ANTENATAL BETAMETHASONE 12 mg IM × 2 doses 24 hr apart for fetal lung maturity (24–34 wk; consider rescue at 24–34 wk if delivery imminent + last dose >14 days ago); DELIVERY is the only definitive treatment — at <34 wk = balance maternal-fetal risk (steroids + delivery at maternal stabilization OR 48 hr later for steroid effect IF maternal-fetal stable); at ≥34 wk = deliver after stabilization; CESAREAN vs vaginal depends on clinical status (no absolute indication for cesarean in HELLP alone but unfavorable cervix + maternal deterioration may prompt cesarean); POSTPARTUM eclampsia risk × 24–48 hr (up to 6 weeks) — continue magnesium × 24 hr postpartum + continued BP monitoring + visit at 7–10 days + 6 weeks; HELLP DIFFERENTIAL = acute fatty liver of pregnancy (AFLP — hypoglycemia + low fibrinogen + elevated ammonia) + thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (TTP — neurologic + pentad + ADAMTS13 <10% + plasma exchange) + atypical hemolytic uremic syndrome (aHUS — predominantly renal + complement dysregulation + eculizumab); FETAL surveillance = NST + BPP + continuous EFM during management; magnesium reduces fetal HR variability (expected) — do not mistake for fetal compromise
