labs.v: BEHAVIORAL + PSYCHOLOGICAL SYMPTOMS OF DEMENTIA (BPSD) — neuropsychiatric symptoms present in 90%+ of dementia patients at some point during disease; INCLUDES: AGITATION (verbal + physical + non-aggressive + aggressive), WANDERING + EXIT-SEEKING, SUNDOWNING (worsening late afternoon/evening), repetitive vocalizations + questions + actions, sleep disturbance (insomnia + nocturnal wandering + reversed sleep-wake cycle), ADL refusal + resistance to care, HALLUCINATIONS (often visual in Lewy body), DELUSIONS (often persecutory + theft), mood disturbance (depression + anxiety + apathy), sexual disinhibition. FRAMEWORK = DICE per American Geriatrics Society + International Psychogeriatric Association: (1) DESCRIBE behavior precisely — WHAT exactly is happening (behavioral specificity not 'difficult' or 'aggressive' generally), WHERE + WHEN + WITH WHOM + FREQUENCY + TRIGGERS + CONSEQUENCES + impact on patient + others; (2) INVESTIGATE underlying causes systematically — medical: pain (PAINAD scale for non-verbal patients), UTI/pneumonia/infection, constipation, dehydration, hunger/thirst, sleep deprivation, medication side effects (especially anticholinergics + benzodiazepines + sedatives + corticosteroids); environmental: noise, lighting, temperature, overstimulation, understimulation, unfamiliar place/people, recent changes; psychological: loneliness, boredom, frustration, fear, unmet need for purpose/dignity/control; (3) CREATE plan with NON-PHARMACOLOGIC FIRST + pharmacologic only if absolutely necessary; (4) EVALUATE response + iterate. NON-PHARMACOLOGIC FIRST-LINE includes: ADDRESS PHYSICAL NEEDS first (toilet + hydration + nutrition + pain control + comfort + sleep + temperature); CONSISTENT caregivers + routine + environment; VALIDATION THERAPY (acknowledge the feeling without correcting facts — 'You miss your home. Tell me about it.' NOT 'Your home was sold 2 years ago'); REMINISCENCE therapy (photos + memories + favorite music + familiar objects); MUSIC THERAPY (often dramatically calming); PET THERAPY + plant care + simple activities; SENSORY interventions (snoezelen rooms + aromatherapy + light therapy for sundowning); ENVIRONMENT modifications (dim lighting + reduced stimulation at sundowning + structured day + activity programming); FAMILY + memory boxes + photos + familiar objects; SIMPLIFIED communication (one direction at a time + slow pace + eye contact + gentle touch + respect); BEDTIME ROUTINE consistency; FALL prevention environmental (low bed + bed alarm + circular walking path + non-skid + clear walking path); DIGNITY + RIGHTS preserved (avoid restraints + offer choices when feasible + respectful tone + privacy + appropriate clothing). PERSON-CENTERED CARE per Tom Kitwood = personhood preserved through relationships + understanding + comfort + occupation + identity + inclusion + love.
labs.r: BPSD is NEUROPSYCHIATRIC manifestation of dementia — common + DRIVEN BY UNMET NEEDS not by 'difficult behavior.' DICE framework systematizes assessment + intervention. NON-PHARMACOLOGIC FIRST + person-centered + addressing unmet needs is gold-standard initial approach. RN role: bedside assessment + non-pharm intervention + de-escalation + advocate for systematic approach + family partnership + dignity preservation.

labs.n: ANTIPSYCHOTICS in dementia — BLACK-BOX warning + when to use carefully
labs.v: FDA BLACK-BOX WARNING for ALL ANTIPSYCHOTICS (typical + atypical) for INCREASED MORTALITY in elderly with DEMENTIA-RELATED PSYCHOSIS — ~1.6× increased death rate over 6–12 weeks of treatment vs placebo (predominantly cardiovascular events + infection); also increased CVA risk; FDA mandates informed consent + reassessment + tapering when possible. NOT FDA-APPROVED for dementia behaviors in US (off-label use); RISPERIDONE approved in EUROPE for short-term aggression in dementia. RESERVED FOR: SEVERE AGITATION + RISK OF HARM to self or others NOT RESPONSIVE TO NON-PHARMACOLOGIC INTERVENTIONS + addressing unmet needs; symptom-targeted (use sparingly + reassess at 4 weeks + plan to taper). PREFERRED AGENTS: (1) QUETIAPINE — lower D2 receptor binding → lower EPS + Parkinsonism risk; start 12.5–25 mg PO QHS; titrate slowly; max usually 100–200 mg daily in dementia; sedating + orthostatic + metabolic + QT considerations; PREFERRED especially in LEWY BODY DEMENTIA + Parkinson's dementia (where typical antipsychotics + risperidone + olanzapine are MORE harmful); (2) RISPERIDONE — 0.25–0.5 mg BID; more EPS at higher doses; some evidence for efficacy in dementia aggression; (3) PIMAVANSERIN (Nuplazid) — FDA-approved for Parkinson disease psychosis (NOT dementia broadly); 5HT2A inverse agonist; less EPS but black-box warning still applies in dementia. AVOID: HALOPERIDOL (high EPS + sedation; only emergent IM if absolutely necessary for safety + brief use); OLANZAPINE (highly anticholinergic + metabolic + sedating in elderly); ARIPIPRAZOLE less common; ZIPRASIDONE QT risk; FIRST-GENERATION typicals (chlorpromazine, thioridazine) avoid (anticholinergic + EPS + cardiac). LEWY BODY DEMENTIA = SEVERE antipsychotic sensitivity → risk of NEUROLEPTIC MALIGNANT SYNDROME (rigidity + hyperthermia + autonomic instability + altered mental status + CK elevation + can be fatal); irreversible Parkinsonism; ALL antipsychotics relatively contraindicated; pimavanserin slightly safer per Parkinson disease psychosis evidence + quetiapine if absolutely needed but with HIGH CAUTION. MONITORING for any antipsychotic in dementia: EFFICACY at 2–4 weeks; metabolic panel + glucose + lipids + ECG (QTc); BP + ortho measurement; EPS + Parkinsonism + dystonia; sedation + falls; mortality watch; FAMILY informed about black-box + benefits-risks. TAPER PLAN: most patients can taper antipsychotic at 3–6 months if symptoms controlled or non-pharm sufficient; ~50% successful taper without relapse; ongoing monitoring. ALTERNATIVES (non-antipsychotic agents — preferred first-line pharmacology if non-pharm insufficient): (1) SSRI — CITALOPRAM 10–20 mg daily for agitation (CitAD trial 2014 showed efficacy comparable to antipsychotics with better safety profile; CAUTION QT prolongation in elderly, max 20 mg daily age >60); sertraline alternative; (2) TRAZODONE 25–100 mg HS for sleep + agitation; (3) GABAPENTIN 100–600 mg HS for agitation + anxiety (off-label); (4) CHOLINESTERASE INHIBITORS (donepezil, rivastigmine, galantamine) — continued through advanced stages may have modest BPSD effect; (5) MEMANTINE (NMDA antagonist) — continued; modest BPSD effect; (6) VALPROATE — off-label for agitation; limited evidence + multiple side effects; rarely used.
labs.r: ANTIPSYCHOTICS in dementia carry FDA BLACK-BOX warning for increased mortality (~1.6×) + CVA + sedation/falls/EPS — reserve for SEVERE agitation + risk of harm not responsive to non-pharm + addressing unmet needs first. PREFERRED quetiapine (lower EPS especially in LBD + Parkinson dementia); risperidone alternative; AVOID haloperidol + olanzapine + first-gen typicals when possible. Lewy body = severe sensitivity. ALTERNATIVES = SSRI/citalopram + trazodone + gabapentin. TAPER at 3–6 months when possible. RN role: educate family + monitor + advocate for non-pharm first.

labs.n: ADVANCED dementia + hospice eligibility + comfort-focused care
labs.v: DEMENTIA STAGING per FAST (Functional Assessment Staging Tool): FAST 1–3 = normal aging to mild cognitive impairment; FAST 4 = mild dementia (difficulty with complex tasks); FAST 5 = moderate (needs help selecting clothing); FAST 6a–e = moderately severe (needs help dressing, bathing, toileting, continence loss, recognition failure); FAST 7a–f = severe (limited vocabulary → 6 words → 1 word → loss of ambulation → loss of trunk control → loss of head control); FAST 7c+ generally hospice-eligible per Medicare hospice benefit (PLUS comorbid medical conditions — aspiration pneumonia, pressure injuries, fractures, dehydration, decline). THIS PATIENT at FAST 6e (continence loss + needs help with all ADLs + recognition impaired but partial — recognizes daughter + close caregivers) — PROGRESSING toward 7a (limited vocabulary). Other staging: GLOBAL DETERIORATION SCALE (GDS), CLINICAL DEMENTIA RATING (CDR), MMSE/MoCA (cognitive screening — FOR THIS PATIENT MMSE 8/30 advanced; MoCA not reliable at this stage). HOSPICE eligibility Medicare guidelines for dementia: FAST 7a OR 7b minimum + 1 or more comorbidities (aspiration pneumonia within past year, pyelonephritis or other upper UTI, septicemia, pressure injury Stage 3 or 4, recurrent fever after antibiotics, inability to maintain sufficient fluid + caloric intake with 10% weight loss in past 6 months or albumin <2.5); also significantly impaired functional capacity. COMFORT-FOCUSED CARE in advanced dementia: (1) GOAL = COMFORT + DIGNITY + QUALITY OF LIFE (not life prolongation or aggressive intervention); (2) NUTRITION + HYDRATION = CAREFUL HAND-FEEDING with respect for cues + dignity + safety (positioning, small bites, supervised, time + patience, favorite foods); NO TUBE FEEDING in advanced dementia per AGS guidance + ASBH + AAHPM consensus — tube feeding does NOT improve survival, function, pressure injury healing, infection risk, or quality of life + INCREASES aspiration pneumonia, agitation needing restraints, distress, pulled tubes; many guidelines consider tube feeding an unethical intervention in advanced dementia; family education central + difficult conversation; (3) NO HOSPITAL TRANSFER unless absolutely necessary for symptom management (hospitalization causes delirium + decline + complications); (4) NO RESUSCITATION (DNR/DNI per advance directive); (5) NO ROUTINE LABS or vital signs as monitoring (only as needed for symptom assessment); (6) AGGRESSIVE SYMPTOM MANAGEMENT — pain (PAINAD scale q4h + scheduled acetaminophen + low-dose opioid if needed for severe pain), agitation (non-pharm first + low-dose pharm if needed), constipation (bowel regimen with all opioids), pressure injury prevention (turning + offloading + skin care + nutrition support), oral care q2-4h with foam swabs + lip balm + avoid glycerin (dries), fall prevention environment; (7) SPIRITUAL + EMOTIONAL care + chaplain + family presence; (8) FAMILY + caregiver support + grief + meaning-making + bereavement; (9) ADVANCE DIRECTIVES + POLST review + honor patient's prior expressed wishes; (10) MEDICATION review + DEPRESCRIBING — many medications no longer benefit in advanced dementia (statins, BP medications may be relaxed, anticoagulants for atrial fibrillation balance, cholinesterase inhibitors questionable in severe disease per AGS Beers + STOPP-START criteria). ETHICS = patient prior expressed wishes + surrogate decision-making + best interest + reasonable medical care + dignity + comfort.
labs.r: ADVANCED dementia care is COMFORT-FOCUSED with FAST 7c+ generally hospice-eligible. NO TUBE FEEDING in advanced dementia per multi-society consensus — does not improve outcomes + causes complications + reduces quality of life. CAREFUL HAND-FEEDING with respect + dignity. Symptom management aggressive. Hospital transfer avoided. Advance directives honored. Medication review + deprescribing. RN role: comfort-focused care + family partnership + dignity preservation + symptom management.

labs.n: SUNDOWNING + DELIRIUM superimposed on dementia (DSD) differential
labs.v: SUNDOWNING = late-afternoon-to-evening worsening of cognitive + behavioral symptoms in dementia (agitation, confusion, anxiety, wandering, calling out, fear) common in moderate-to-severe dementia; circadian rhythm disruption + fatigue + reduced sensory cues at dusk + caregiver shift change + reduced staff + activity reduction + light reduction + understimulation/overstimulation; PREVENTION-FIRST approach: GRADUAL DIM LIGHTING (not abrupt darkness — circadian cue); MAINTAIN ROUTINE through transition periods; LIGHT THERAPY in morning (10,000 lux 30 min); STRUCTURED ACTIVITY during day; ADEQUATE SLEEP at night (avoid daytime napping >30 min); REDUCED STIMULATION in evening (quiet music + low TV volume + dim lights + soft lighting + warm environment); FAMILIAR objects + photos visible; FAMILY phone call or visit in afternoon if possible; CONSISTENT staff during transitions; AVOID overstimulation activities late afternoon; ADEQUATE NUTRITION + HYDRATION not too late (avoid full bladder at night); BEDTIME RITUAL consistent (warm milk + favorite quilt + soft music + dim lights + reading + back rub + verbal comfort). DELIRIUM SUPERIMPOSED ON DEMENTIA (DSD) — separate diagnosis to RULE OUT in any acute behavioral change in dementia patient: acute onset over hours to days + FLUCTUATING course + INATTENTION + altered consciousness + disorganized thinking; CONTRIBUTORS = infection (UTI most common in elderly + pneumonia), pain, medication (anticholinergics, benzodiazepines, opioids, polypharmacy), constipation, urinary retention, dehydration, electrolytes (especially sodium), hypoxia, hypoglycemia, postoperative, recent hospitalization, sleep deprivation, sensory deprivation. CAM (Confusion Assessment Method) = acute onset + inattention + EITHER disorganized thinking OR altered consciousness; CAM-ICU + bCAM for inpatient. WORKUP: full medical workup including UA + UC + CBC + CMP + glucose + medication review + consider chest X-ray + EKG + neuro exam + symptom history. TREATMENT: address underlying cause + non-pharm delirium prevention (HELP bundle — orientation, hearing/vision, mobility, hydration/nutrition, sleep, family, avoid Foley, avoid restraints, address pain, medication review); pharmacologic ONLY for safety with low-dose antipsychotic (quetiapine 12.5–25 mg, haloperidol 0.25–0.5 mg if needed); AVOID benzodiazepines (worsen delirium, except alcohol withdrawal/seizure); MOBILIZE early. DSD has WORSE outcomes than delirium alone or dementia alone — accelerated cognitive decline + higher mortality. THIS PATIENT today: increased agitation past 6 weeks → SUSPECT delirium superimposed on chronic BPSD; workup pending (UA + CBC + CMP + medication review + glucose + abdominal exam). Pattern of agitation = sundowning + behavioral incident with triggers identified = some component of routine BPSD; full workup will confirm.
labs.r: SUNDOWNING is COMMON in dementia + PREVENTABLE with environmental + routine modifications. DSD is SEPARATE diagnosis + requires workup + worsens outcomes. DICE workup includes DSD rule-out. Non-pharm interventions for both. AVOID benzodiazepines (worsen delirium). RN role: recognize patterns + workup + non-pharm intervention + escalation if needed.

labs.n: PAIN assessment in dementia — PAINAD + behavioral cues
labs.v: PAIN in advanced dementia is COMMONLY UNDER-RECOGNIZED + UNDER-TREATED because patient may not verbalize. ESTIMATED 50% of dementia patients have pain not reported. CHRONIC pain sources common in elderly: osteoarthritis (joint stiffness + decreased mobility), chronic low back pain, neuropathic pain (diabetic, post-herpetic), headache, dental, GI (constipation, GERD), skin (pressure injuries, dermatitis), musculoskeletal post-fall. RECOGNITION via BEHAVIORAL CUES = agitation + restlessness + facial grimacing + guarding + rubbing + decreased appetite + sleep disturbance + verbal vocalizations + crying + change in baseline behavior + resistance to ADLs + reduced participation. PAINAD scale (Pain Assessment in Advanced Dementia) — 5 items (breathing + negative vocalization + facial expression + body language + consolability) each 0–2 = total 0–10; 1–3 mild + 4–6 moderate + 7–10 severe. OTHER scales: Abbey Pain Scale, DOLOPLUS-2, NOPPAIN, PACSLAC. ASSESSMENT q4h or with any behavioral change. SCHEDULED ACETAMINOPHEN 500 mg PO q6h (max 3 g/day in elderly + adjust for liver function) is FIRST-LINE for mild-moderate pain in dementia — often dramatically improves agitation + BPSD when pain was unrecognized contributor; trial × 2 weeks before adding opioid. SECOND-LINE = TOPICAL NSAIDs (diclofenac gel, capsaicin) for localized; AVOID oral NSAIDs in elderly (GI + renal + cardiovascular per Beers criteria — relative). OPIOID for moderate-severe pain not controlled by acetaminophen: start LOW-DOSE morphine 2.5 mg PO/SQ q4h PRN or hydromorphone 0.5 mg q4h PRN; monitor for sedation + constipation (bowel regimen with all opioids); titrate to comfort; AVOID meperidine (CNS toxicity in elderly), tramadol (serotonin syndrome risk + seizure threshold), oxycodone (somewhat preferred over morphine in renal impairment). ADJUVANTS for neuropathic pain: gabapentin, pregabalin, duloxetine (limited use in elderly + renal adjustment + sedation considerations). NON-PHARMACOLOGIC pain management: positioning, warm/cool packs, massage, music, distraction, gentle touch, ROM stretches, repositioning q2h, pressure offloading. PAIN treatment SHOULD BE CONSIDERED FIRST when working through DICE for BPSD — undertreated pain is a common reversible cause of agitation in dementia.
labs.r: PAIN in dementia is UNDER-RECOGNIZED + UNDER-TREATED — use PAINAD or other behavioral scales. Scheduled ACETAMINOPHEN first-line for mild-moderate pain + often dramatically improves BPSD. Topical NSAIDs alternative for localized. Opioid for moderate-severe pain with caution + bowel regimen. AVOID NSAIDs oral, meperidine, tramadol in elderly. PAIN should be considered FIRST in DICE workup of BPSD. RN role: behavioral assessment + scheduled pain medication + non-pharm pain interventions.

labs.n: FAMILY education + caregiver support + advance care planning
labs.v: FAMILY education + caregiver support are central in dementia care + impact patient outcomes + family wellbeing. PROGRESSION expected of dementia — family understanding of disease trajectory + stages + symptoms + decline + life expectancy (average ~8 years from Alzheimer's diagnosis but varies widely; FAST 7 typically 1–2 years life expectancy; cessation of feeding + comorbidities accelerate decline). EARLY discussions of advance care planning + advance directives + POLST when patient still has decisional capacity (HIGHEST priority — this patient completed at FAST 5 with decisional capacity; we now honor those wishes). PROGRESSIVE shifts in care goals: early = MAINTENANCE + cognitive enhancement + behavioral support; middle = SUPPORT + ADL assistance + safety + caregiver education; advanced = COMFORT-FOCUSED + dignity + end-of-life planning + hospice consideration. FAMILY MEETINGS at key transitions — diagnosis + functional decline + nursing home admission + advanced stage + hospice transition + end-of-life. CAREGIVER FATIGUE = recognized health issue with increased morbidity + mortality (depression + cardiovascular + immunosuppression + decreased quality of life); SUPPORT = caregiver support groups (Alzheimer's Association + local), respite care, day programs, in-home help (IHSS in CA + Medi-Cal), counseling, online communities, hotline 800-272-3900 (Alzheimer's Association 24/7); ANTICIPATORY GRIEF + recognition (grief begins long before death — 'long goodbye'); CAREGIVER decision-making for tube feeding + hospitalization + treatment intensity + EOL care = difficult emotional + ethical territory + family meetings facilitate + chaplain + social work + ethics consult available. PERSON-CENTERED CARE per Tom Kitwood = preserve PERSONHOOD through relationships + understanding + comfort + occupation + identity + inclusion + love; family role essential. HOSPICE TRANSITION discussions when FAST 7+ + comorbidities + decline trajectory + comfort-focused goals; benefits = comprehensive interdisciplinary care + symptom management + family support + caregiver respite + bereavement; not 'giving up' but appropriate care. POST-DEATH bereavement support per Medicare hospice benefit 13 months + grief support + commemoration. THIS PATIENT family meeting outcomes: daughter informed + engaged + supportive of non-pharm first-line; daughter signed updated POLST reaffirming DNR/DNI/AND + no tube feeding + comfort-focused; daughter in caregiver support group × 6 months; daughter visiting weekly Saturdays × 3-4 hr + daily phone calls; chaplain + social work continued involvement; hospice eligibility evaluation planned at next decline (FAST 7a/b/c); ETHICS consult available if family conflict or difficult decisions; PATIENT VOICE preserved through prior advance directives + ongoing observation of preferences (food choice + comfort + family presence).
labs.r: FAMILY education + caregiver support + advance care planning are central in dementia care. Disease trajectory understanding. Caregiver fatigue recognition + support. ADVANCE DIRECTIVES + POLST early when patient has capacity. Family meetings at key transitions. Hospice consideration at FAST 7+. Person-centered care preserves personhood. RN role: facilitate meetings + provide education + emotional support + advocate for patient wishes.

labs.n: MEDICATION review + DEPRESCRIBING in advanced dementia
labs.v: MEDICATION REVIEW + DEPRESCRIBING is essential in advanced dementia per AGS Beers criteria + STOPP-START criteria. PRINCIPLES: (1) goals of care + life expectancy + benefits-risks of each medication; (2) time-to-benefit vs life expectancy (statins for primary prevention — life expectancy <5 years generally too short for benefit; secondary prevention may continue if low side effects); (3) anticholinergic burden (cumulative anticholinergic medications worsen cognition + delirium + falls + dry mouth + constipation + urinary retention); (4) sedatives + benzodiazepines (Beers criteria avoid in elderly — falls + delirium + cognitive decline); (5) NSAIDs (GI + renal + cardiovascular + bleeding risk); (6) sliding-scale insulin alone (Beers avoid — hyperglycemia better managed with longer-acting basal + as-needed correction); (7) anticoagulation for AFib — balance stroke prevention vs falls + bleeding + advanced age + advanced dementia + life expectancy + functional status (sometimes deprescribed in advanced dementia + falls + comfort focus); (8) BP medications — relaxed targets in elderly (SBP <140–150 acceptable) + falls + ortho hypo considerations; (9) cholinesterase inhibitors (donepezil, rivastigmine, galantamine) + memantine — modest BPSD effect + cognitive maintenance + continue in moderate dementia; some guidelines recommend D/C in advanced (FAST 7+) if no clear benefit + side effects; (10) PRN psychotropics — review necessity + use + side effects. SCHEDULED ACETAMINOPHEN as analgesic worth trying in BPSD patients — often dramatically improves agitation + reduces need for antipsychotics. CONSULT pharmacy + geriatric medicine for systematic review. THIS PATIENT current medications + review: DONEPEZIL 10 mg daily (continued — modest benefit; reassess at FAST 7); MEMANTINE 10 mg BID (continued — modest benefit; reassess at FAST 7); LISINOPRIL 10 mg daily (continued — HTN + relaxed target; monitor ortho); AMLODIPINE 5 mg daily (continued — HTN); ATORVASTATIN 40 mg daily (consider deprescribing per goals of care + advanced dementia + limited life expectancy — discussion with family); SERTRALINE 50 mg daily (continued — depression + anxiety; alternative for BPSD per CitAD though citalopram studied not sertraline); TRAZODONE 50 mg HS (continued — sleep + nocturnal agitation; well-tolerated + lower anticholinergic than older antidepressants); ACETAMINOPHEN 500 mg PO q6h scheduled (continued — first-line pain in BPSD); MULTIVITAMIN + vitamin D + calcium (continued — bone health + supplementation); PRN HALOPERIDOL 0.5 mg IM (held not used × 8 weeks — consider DEPRESCRIBE given non-use + black-box; replace with PRN QUETIAPINE 12.5–25 mg PO at bedtime if needed) + PRN LORAZEPAM 0.5 mg PO/IM (held not used × 8 weeks — consider DEPRESCRIBE given benzodiazepine risks in elderly + alternatives). PROPOSED CHANGES: replace haloperidol PRN with quetiapine PRN (lower EPS + per institutional protocol); discontinue lorazepam PRN (Beers + alternative non-pharm + trazodone HS already on board); consider statin discussion with daughter at family meeting; continue all other medications.
labs.r: MEDICATION REVIEW + DEPRESCRIBING per AGS Beers + STOPP-START is essential in advanced dementia. Goals of care + life expectancy + benefits-risks per medication. Anticholinergic + sedative burden reduction. SCHEDULED ACETAMINOPHEN worth trying. Pharmacy + geriatric consult valuable. RN role: medication review + advocate + family education + monitor + reassess.

labs.v: PERTINENT NEGATIVES: no acute UTI (UA clean); no pneumonia (lungs clear + SpO2 normal + no fever); no constipation (BM yesterday + soft); no dehydration severe (mild on exam + UOP 800 mL/24h slightly low; will increase oral hydration + monitor); no acute pain on PAINAD 1/10; no fall × 14 months (preventive interventions effective); no pressure injuries (Stage 0 — Braden 16 acceptable); no recent hospitalization (favors continued stable course); no acute mood crisis (sertraline + trazodone effective); no suicidal ideation (advanced dementia + limited communication but no observable distress signs); no abuse or neglect concerns (Memory Care unit accredited + RN/LVN/CNA ratios appropriate + daughter engaged + frequent visits + chaplain involvement); no medication adverse effects current; no acute medical illness; no falls; no delirium currently (CAM negative — chronic stable BPSD pattern not acute fluctuation); no concerning weight loss in past 2 weeks (4 kg over 4 months is gradual + monitored). PROTECTIVE FACTORS: ENGAGED DAUGHTER as healthcare proxy + frequent contact + advocate; ADVANCE DIRECTIVES + POLST in place + recently updated; comfort-focused goals clearly articulated; accredited Memory Care Unit with experienced staff + DICE framework + non-pharm-first approach institutional culture; pharmacy + geriatric medicine consultative; chaplain + social worker available; previous occupational success + identity (former 3rd grade teacher × 38 years — reminiscence anchor); favorite quilt + photos + grandfather clock from home (familiar environment); responsive to validation therapy + music + reminiscence (de-escalation effective); intact sleep with trazodone; nutrition stable; no concerning labs; no acute medical issues; no antipsychotic side effects (PRN held); supportive family circle (church friends + neighbors visit occasionally); insurance coverage + Medicare hospice benefit eligible when indicated; family in caregiver support group; chaplain involved + spiritual care continuity.
labs.r: Multiple PROTECTIVE FACTORS support good ongoing care + appropriate symptom management without antipsychotics + comfort-focused approach + dignity-preserving environment. Standard expected stable advanced dementia trajectory with appropriate non-pharm-first + family-engaged + person-centered care. Disease progression continues (FAST 6e → 7 over coming months-years) + hospice eligibility evaluation will be revisited.

nursesNote: Day 0 0700 — 82 y/o female R. Whitaker, Memory Care Unit Bed 14 resident × 2 years, advanced Alzheimer's disease FAST 6e (continence loss + needs help with all ADLs + partial recognition — recognizes daughter + close caregivers; MMSE 8/30 advanced); admitted to facility after husband's death 26 months ago; daughter M. Whitaker (only child + healthcare proxy) primary advocate + visits Saturdays × 3-4 hr + daily phone calls. PMH: Alzheimer's disease (diagnosed 2017; gradual decline; FAST stage tracking); HTN (lisinopril + amlodipine; relaxed SBP target <140 per elderly geriatric guidance); hypercholesterolemia (atorvastatin — discussion with daughter at family meeting re: deprescribing per advanced dementia + limited life expectancy); depression + anxiety (sertraline + trazodone — adequate response × 18 months); chronic OA (acetaminophen scheduled). ALLERGIES = SULFA (rash) + FLUOXETINE (severe agitation 2 yr ago tried for depression — documented contraindication). CODE STATUS = DNR/DNI per advance directive completed at FAST 5 with decisional capacity (18 months ago); recently updated POLST = AND + DNR/DNI + no artificial nutrition/hydration unless symptom relief + comfort-focused care + no hospital transfer unless absolutely necessary for symptom management. CURRENT MEDICATIONS: donepezil 10 mg PO daily + memantine 10 mg PO BID + lisinopril 10 mg PO daily + amlodipine 5 mg PO daily + atorvastatin 40 mg PO daily + sertraline 50 mg PO daily + trazodone 50 mg PO HS + ACETAMINOPHEN 500 mg PO q6h scheduled (first-line BPSD pain trial — effective × 6 months) + multivitamin + vit D + calcium; PRN haloperidol 0.5 mg IM (held × 8 weeks — consider deprescribe + replace with quetiapine PRN) + PRN lorazepam 0.5 mg PO/IM (held × 8 weeks — consider deprescribe per Beers + alternatives). PRESENTING ISSUE: progressively severe BPSD over past 6 weeks (agitation + wandering + sundowning + repetitive vocalizations + sleep disturbance + ADL refusal — escalating from baseline mild-moderate BPSD); family meeting scheduled 1500 today re: management approach + advance care + hospice eligibility evaluation. VITALS 0700: HR 78 NSR, BP 138/82 MAP 101 (on antihypertensives + relaxed target), RR 16, SpO2 97% RA, T 36.7°C; weight 52 kg (DOWN 4 kg over past 4 months — gradual decline + decreased appetite; BMI 19 from 21 baseline; mild dehydration on physical exam + UOP 800 mL/24h yesterday — will increase oral hydration + monitor; trending toward malnutrition + dehydration but not severe yet); patient awake + restless + pacing in room + occasionally calling 'where am I?' + sundowning agitation from yesterday continues; declined breakfast initially × 30 min then ate 50% with hand-feeding assistance; toileting partially independent with prompting + cuing. BEHAVIORAL INCIDENT 1045: patient became agitated → walked into another resident's room + pulled at her grandfather clock cord trying to 'fix it' + told staff person 'leave me alone' + attempted to leave through fire exit door + activated bed alarm. RN-led DICE FRAMEWORK assessment: (1) DESCRIBE = agitation + wandering + door-seeking + verbal protest + intrusion into other resident's room; (2) INVESTIGATE potential triggers — morning shift change at 1045 + new unfamiliar caregiver assigned + breakfast tray taken away earlier than usual + grandfather clock chime louder than expected this morning + last urination 0815 (3 hr ago); medical workup activated: UA sent (urinary urgency suspected → 250 mL urine + relief on patient face after toileting; UA results clean = NO UTI); CBC + CMP sent (rule out occult illness; results pending); medication review with pharmacy + geriatric medicine = no recent changes + no acute side effects; abdominal exam soft + non-distended + last BM yesterday (no constipation); pain assessment PAINAD 1/10 currently (mild — no significant pain); environmental review + interventions planned; (3) CREATE plan with non-pharmacologic FIRST = consistent caregiver assignment in afternoon + clock chime volume reduced + bedtime ritual maintained + sundowning prep + validation therapy + reminiscence + favorite music + family phone call scheduled; (4) EVALUATE: de-escalation effective within 12 min with calm voice + offer of warm tea + reminiscence about teaching career (former 3rd grade teacher × 38 yr — strong identity anchor); vital signs normalized over 30 min; behavior documented + plan refined; no PRN antipsychotic used (held). FAMILY MEETING 1500 in family room with patient (limited participation given dementia stage but present + accompanied by daughter) + daughter + attending physician + Memory Care RN + chaplain + social worker: REVIEWED BPSD progression + interventions to date + DICE framework + non-pharmacologic care plan that worked yesterday (sundowning de-escalated without PRN); DISCUSSED ANTIPSYCHOTIC RISKS — FDA BLACK-BOX WARNING all antipsychotics (typical + atypical) for INCREASED MORTALITY in elderly with dementia-related psychosis (~1.6× over 6–12 weeks) + sedation + falls + EPS + ortho hypo + LBD severe sensitivity (not LBD in this patient — Alzheimer's) — RESERVED FOR severe agitation + risk of harm not responsive to non-pharm + addressing unmet needs first; PREFERRED LOW-DOSE QUETIAPINE 12.5–25 mg PO QHS PRN (lower D2 → lower EPS + sedation manageable + ortho monitoring) IF non-pharm fails; 4-week reassessment + plan to taper at 3–6 months when possible; ALTERNATIVES discussed — SSRI (sertraline already on; consider citalopram per CitAD evidence — caution QT prolongation + max 20 mg age >60), trazodone HS already on board, gabapentin (off-label) considered + held; DEPRESCRIBING discussed — haloperidol PRN deprescribe (replace with quetiapine PRN) + lorazepam PRN deprescribe (Beers + alternatives + benzodiazepines worsen delirium + falls risk); STATIN discussion with daughter (atorvastatin 40 mg daily — primary prevention + limited life expectancy + advanced dementia → discuss D/C; daughter receptive + agreed to D/C per relaxed goals + reduced pill burden + reduced LFT monitoring need + reduced side effects risk); DISCUSSED advance care + comfort-focused care + hospice eligibility evaluation (FAST 6e currently; will reassess at FAST 7a/b/c for hospice consideration; criteria FAST 7+ AND ≥1 comorbidity like aspiration pneumonia + pressure injury Stage 3-4 + recurrent UTI + recurrent fever after antibiotics + inability to maintain nutrition with 10% weight loss in past 6 months or albumin <2.5; this patient 4 kg / 6 months = 7% loss approaching threshold; will trend); DISCUSSED TUBE FEEDING NOT RECOMMENDED in advanced dementia per multi-society consensus (AGS + ASBH + AAHPM) — does NOT improve survival, function, pressure injury healing, infection risk, or quality of life; INCREASES aspiration pneumonia + agitation needing restraints + distress + pulled tubes; CAREFUL HAND-FEEDING with respect for cues + dignity + safety (positioning, small bites, supervised, time + patience, favorite foods) is the standard; daughter reaffirmed advance directive against tube feeding; GOALS reinforced — comfort + dignity + family presence + maintain function as long as possible + avoid hospital + avoid restraints + avoid tube feeding; CHAPLAIN provided spiritual support + prayer + presence; SOCIAL WORK confirmed daughter in caregiver support group × 6 months + offered additional respite + crisis line; FAMILY education on disease trajectory + life expectancy + person-centered care + bereavement preparation when ready (anticipatory grief acknowledged); UPDATED POLST signed by daughter today reaffirming current goals. ENVIRONMENTAL PREPARATION for sundowning 1430 onward: gradual lighting reduction from full → warm dim, soft music (her preferred 1950s big band — Glenn Miller + Tommy Dorsey + favorite radio station), familiar quilt from home, photos visible, family room offered + accepted briefly; reminiscence with RN about teaching memories; supper served early at 1530 with favorite foods (chicken soup + warm milk + ice cream — comfort foods her mother used to make) + tea; bedtime ritual planned for 1900 with warm milk + dim lights + favorite quilt + chiming clock + back rub + reading + chaplain visit if desired; daughter scheduled to call at 1600 for daily phone visit; chair alarm + bed alarm + bed in low position for fall prevention; circular walking path available + supervised. SUNDOWNING EPISODE 1700–1830 (90 min): patient calling 'I want to go home' + restless pacing → de-escalation by 1830 with VALIDATION THERAPY ('You miss your home. You loved your home. Tell me about it' — NOT 'Your home was sold 2 years ago') + presence + warm milk + favorite quilt + soft music + RN sat with patient + held her hand + spoke calmly + reminisced about teaching career + offered toilet + light snack + sequenced bedtime routine; agitation resolved gradually + patient calm + settling for bedtime; no PRN medications used; daughter phone call at 1600 happy + reassuring; daughter expressed appreciation for non-pharm approach + 'I don't want her on those Haldol-type pills'; documentation completed; team huddle at change of shift to review effective interventions + carry plan forward. Day 1 0700 update: restful night with non-pharm bedtime routine; ate breakfast 50% with prompting; family meeting outcomes implemented (replaced haloperidol PRN with quetiapine 12.5 mg PO HS PRN order + D/C lorazepam PRN + D/C atorvastatin per daughter consent + continue all other meds + continue scheduled acetaminophen + continue cholinesterase inhibitor + memantine + sertraline + trazodone + multivitamin + vit D + calcium); daughter visit Saturday afternoon as scheduled; chaplain visit weekly; care plan posted in chart + room + communicated to all staff. PLAN ongoing: DICE-driven non-pharm first-line for BPSD; PAINAD q4h + with any behavioral change + treat pain; address unmet needs systematically (toilet + hydration + nutrition + pain + comfort + sleep + sensory + social + environment); validation + reminiscence + music + family + familiar objects + consistent routine + person-centered care; PRN QUETIAPINE 12.5–25 mg PO HS only if severe agitation not responsive to non-pharm + risk of harm; weekly multidisciplinary care planning meetings; monthly family check-in calls (in addition to weekly visits + daily calls); reassess hospice eligibility quarterly + sooner if decline; bereavement preparation + caregiver support continued; daughter contact + support + respite resources + grief support; team well-being + EAP + chaplain + peer support for staff.
providerOrders: RESIDE in Memory Care Unit Bed 14 (single room with familiar objects + dementia-friendly environment + chair alarm + bed alarm + bed in low position + circular walking path supervised)

DIAGNOSIS: advanced Alzheimer's disease FAST stage 6e progressing toward 7 with severe BPSD (agitation + wandering + sundowning + repetitive vocalizations + sleep disturbance + ADL refusal); chronic HTN; depression + anxiety; chronic OA pain; mild dehydration; gradual weight loss

CODE STATUS: DNR/DNI per advance directive (completed at FAST 5 with decisional capacity); POLST = AND + DNR/DNI + no artificial nutrition/hydration unless symptom relief + comfort-focused care + no hospital transfer unless absolutely necessary
BPSD MANAGEMENT — DICE framework non-pharmacologic FIRST:
DESCRIBE behavior precisely with team + family input
INVESTIGATE underlying causes — pain (PAINAD) + UTI + pneumonia + constipation + dehydration + hunger + thirst + sleep + sensory + medication + environment + caregiver + triggers

CREATE plan with non-pharm interventions: address physical needs first + consistent caregivers + routine + environment + validation therapy + reminiscence + music + family + familiar objects + simplified communication + bedtime ritual + sundowning prep + person-centered care + dignity
EVALUATE response + iterate
PAINAD scale q4h + with any behavioral change + scheduled acetaminophen for pain
Workup for delirium superimposed on dementia (DSD) — CAM + UA + CBC + CMP + medication review + glucose + abdominal exam
PRN PHARMACOLOGIC (only if non-pharm fails + severe agitation + risk of harm):

QUETIAPINE 12.5–25 mg PO HS PRN — replaces previous haloperidol PRN (lower D2 → lower EPS + sedation manageable + ortho monitoring; FDA BLACK-BOX warning for increased mortality in elderly dementia — used sparingly + 4-week reassessment + plan to taper at 3–6 months)
DISCONTINUED haloperidol PRN (high EPS + sedation; replaced with quetiapine PRN per geriatric protocol)
DISCONTINUED lorazepam PRN (Beers criteria + alternatives + benzodiazepine worsens delirium + falls risk)
Monitor for EPS + Parkinsonism + sedation + ortho hypo + QT prolongation + metabolic side effects if quetiapine used
SCHEDULED MEDICATIONS:
Donepezil 10 mg PO daily (continued — modest BPSD effect + cognitive maintenance)
Memantine 10 mg PO BID (continued — modest BPSD effect)
Lisinopril 10 mg PO daily (HTN; relaxed SBP <140 target per elderly)
Amlodipine 5 mg PO daily (HTN)
Sertraline 50 mg PO daily (depression + anxiety; consider future switch to citalopram per CitAD trial for BPSD agitation — caution QT in elderly + max 20 mg)
Trazodone 50 mg PO HS (sleep + nocturnal agitation; well-tolerated in elderly)
ACETAMINOPHEN 500 mg PO q6h scheduled (max 3 g/day in elderly + LFT monitoring — first-line pain in BPSD)
Multivitamin + vitamin D 1000 IU + calcium 600 mg daily
DISCONTINUED atorvastatin 40 mg daily (per daughter consent + advanced dementia + limited life expectancy + relaxed goals + reduced pill burden + per AGS Beers/STOPP-START + deprescribing principles)
PAIN MANAGEMENT (often dramatically improves BPSD):
Scheduled acetaminophen 500 mg PO q6h (first-line)
Topical NSAIDs (diclofenac gel) for localized joint pain as needed
Low-dose opioid (morphine 2.5 mg PO/SQ q4h PRN) for severe pain not controlled by acetaminophen — with bowel regimen
AVOID oral NSAIDs (Beers — GI + renal + cardiovascular)
AVOID meperidine + tramadol (CNS toxicity + serotonin syndrome + seizure in elderly)
PAINAD scale q4h + with any behavioral change
Non-pharmacologic — positioning + warm/cool packs + massage + music + ROM + repositioning
ENVIRONMENTAL + non-pharm care:
Familiar objects from home (favorite quilt + photos + grandfather clock — but reduce chime volume to lower stimulus)
Consistent caregivers in afternoon + transition periods
Routine maintenance + structured day + activity programming
Validation therapy (acknowledge feeling not facts)
Reminiscence therapy (teaching career + photos + memories + music)
Music therapy (1950s big band + favorite radio station)
Bedtime ritual consistent (warm milk + dim lights + favorite quilt + soft music + reading + back rub)
Sundowning prep 1430+ (gradual dim lighting + reduced stimulation + soft music + family room availability + reminiscence + early supper + bedtime ritual approach)
Light therapy in morning if available (10,000 lux 30 min)
Fall prevention environment (low bed + bed alarm + chair alarm + non-skid + clear walking path + circular path supervised)
Family room available + visits supported
AVOID restraints (not appropriate in dementia care + worsens agitation)
AVOID Foley unless absolutely indicated (UTI risk + agitation + restraint-like)
NUTRITION + HYDRATION (comfort + dignity-focused):
CAREFUL HAND-FEEDING with respect for cues + dignity + safety (positioning, small bites, supervised, time + patience, favorite foods)
NO TUBE FEEDING per advance directive + multi-society consensus (does not improve outcomes + increases aspiration + complications + reduces quality of life in advanced dementia)
Favorite foods (chicken soup + warm milk + ice cream + soft foods + comfort foods)
Hydration 1500–2000 mL/day target (offer fluids frequently + various forms — water + juice + tea + soup + popsicles + ice chips + favorite drinks)
Monitor weight weekly + UOP + dehydration signs (mucous membranes + skin turgor + UOP + orthostasis)
Nutrition consult if continued weight loss
Vitamin D + multivitamin daily
Oral care q2-4h with foam swabs + saline + lip balm (avoid glycerin — dries)
MONITORING:
Vitals q shift (more frequent if acute behavioral change or illness signs)
Daily weights
Weekly skin assessment + Braden score
PAINAD q4h + with behavioral change
Behavioral charting + DICE framework documentation
Sleep + appetite + ADL participation + mood + social engagement tracked
Falls risk reassessed monthly + after any change
Medication review by pharmacy + geriatric medicine quarterly
Goals of care + hospice eligibility reassessment quarterly + with any decline
FAMILY + interdisciplinary:
Daughter (healthcare proxy) weekly Saturday visits + daily phone calls + accessible by phone 24/7
Daughter in caregiver support group × 6 months (continued + supported)
Family meeting quarterly + with any significant change or decision
Chaplain weekly + as needed (patient identifies as Methodist)
Social worker monthly + as needed
Geriatric medicine consult quarterly + as needed
Pharmacy consult quarterly + with medication changes
Activity therapy + recreation programs ongoing
Music therapy weekly group + individual sessions
Pet therapy weekly (resident facility dog visits)
Spiritual care + community church group occasionally
Hospice eligibility evaluation quarterly + with decline
Bereavement preparation when ready (anticipatory grief acknowledged + supported)
TEAM EDUCATION + support:
DICE framework training for all Memory Care staff
Person-centered care principles + Kitwood approach
Validation therapy + reminiscence + music therapy techniques
Recognizing + managing BPSD without antipsychotic reliance
Caregiver fatigue + self-care + EAP + peer support
Compassionate care + dignity preservation
Documentation + communication + handoff
Family + cultural + spiritual sensitivity
INSTITUTIONAL safety + quality:
Antipsychotic prescription monitoring (per CMS quality measure for nursing home antipsychotic use)
Behavioral incident review + lessons learned + protocol refinement
Restraint-free care (no physical or chemical restraints except in true emergencies + per regulation)
Falls prevention + post-fall huddle (no falls × 14 months — interventions effective)
Pressure injury prevention bundle
Nutrition + hydration + ADL care planning
Person-centered care quality metric

steps.title: Recognize Cues
steps.sub: What matters in this BPSD episode + ongoing care?
steps.inst: Select ALL findings that are clinically + behaviorally significant cues requiring nursing attention.

opts.text: BPSD pattern over past 6 weeks — progressively severe agitation + wandering + sundowning + repetitive vocalizations + sleep disturbance + ADL refusal in patient with advanced Alzheimer's FAST 6e; today's incident at 1045 = agitation + door-seeking + intrusion into other resident's room + verbal protest; DICE framework needed for systematic assessment + intervention; underlying UNMET NEEDS driving behavior (not 'difficult behavior')
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — BPSD recognition + DICE framework as foundation. BPSD is neuropsychiatric manifestation of dementia + driven by unmet needs. Systematic Describe + Investigate + Create + Evaluate approach addresses root causes. Foundation for non-pharm-first management.

opts.text: DICE workup — DESCRIBE (agitation + wandering + door-seeking + verbal protest 1045–1115); INVESTIGATE potential triggers (morning shift change + new unfamiliar caregiver + breakfast tray taken early + grandfather clock chime louder than usual + urinary urgency 3 hr since last toileting) + medical workup (UA clean = no UTI; CBC + CMP pending; abdominal exam soft + last BM yesterday = no constipation; PAINAD 1/10 = no significant pain; medication review unchanged); CREATE non-pharm plan + de-escalate; EVALUATE response (de-escalated within 12 min)
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — DICE workup. Systematic assessment identifies multiple modifiable triggers (caregiver, environment, urination need). Non-pharm intervention effective. RN role: facilitate systematic approach + address each unmet need.

opts.text: AVOID ANTIPSYCHOTICS when possible per FDA BLACK-BOX WARNING all antipsychotics for INCREASED MORTALITY in elderly with dementia-related psychosis (~1.6× increased death rate over 6–12 weeks); also sedation + falls + EPS + ortho hypo + QT + metabolic + LBD severe sensitivity; PRN haloperidol held × 8 weeks → replaced with PRN quetiapine 12.5–25 mg PO HS (lower EPS); 4-week reassessment + plan to taper at 3–6 months
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — antipsychotic risks + appropriate use. FDA black-box warning for elderly dementia mandates informed consent + reassessment + tapering. Reserved for severe agitation + risk of harm not responsive to non-pharm. Quetiapine preferred for elderly + dementia (lower D2 → lower EPS especially in LBD). Alternatives = SSRI/citalopram + trazodone + gabapentin. Frequently tested NCLEX safety topic.

opts.text: PAIN ASSESSMENT in dementia using PAINAD scale q4h + with any behavioral change — pain is COMMONLY UNDER-RECOGNIZED in dementia + driver of BPSD; SCHEDULED acetaminophen 500 mg PO q6h first-line (often dramatically improves BPSD when pain unrecognized contributor); topical NSAIDs for localized; low-dose opioid for severe; AVOID oral NSAIDs + meperidine + tramadol in elderly (Beers)
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — pain assessment in dementia. PAINAD behavioral scale + scheduled acetaminophen first-line + trial × 2 weeks before adding opioid. Pain treatment often dramatically improves agitation. Pain consideration should be FIRST in DICE workup.

opts.text: FAMILY MEETING 1500 with daughter (healthcare proxy) + attending physician + Memory Care RN + chaplain + social worker re: BPSD progression + management plan + advance care + hospice eligibility evaluation; reaffirmed POLST (DNR/DNI/AND + no artificial nutrition + comfort-focused + no hospital transfer); deprescribed atorvastatin per advanced dementia + relaxed goals; replaced haloperidol PRN with quetiapine PRN; family supportive + non-pharm-first; chaplain spiritual support; daughter in caregiver support group × 6 months
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — family-centered care + advance care planning. Healthcare proxy partnership. Advance directives honored. Medication review + deprescribing per goals of care. Hospice eligibility evaluation. Chaplain + social work + interdisciplinary engagement. Family support throughout.

opts.text: ENVIRONMENTAL + non-pharmacologic care: familiar objects from home (favorite quilt + photos + grandfather clock); consistent caregivers + routine + transition periods; validation therapy + reminiscence + music + favorite foods; sundowning prep 1430+ (gradual dim lighting + reduced stimulation + family room + bedtime ritual approach); fall prevention environment (low bed + bed alarm + supervised circular path); AVOID restraints + Foley unless indicated
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — environmental + non-pharm care is the foundation. Familiar objects + consistent caregivers + routine + validation + reminiscence + music + sundowning prep + bedtime ritual + fall prevention + avoiding restraints all contribute to person-centered care + reduced BPSD.

opts.text: ADVANCED dementia care — FAST 6e progressing toward 7; COMFORT-FOCUSED CARE; CAREFUL HAND-FEEDING with respect for cues + dignity + safety; NO TUBE FEEDING per advance directive + multi-society consensus (AGS + ASBH + AAHPM — does not improve outcomes + increases aspiration + complications in advanced dementia); hospice eligibility evaluation quarterly + with decline (FAST 7+ + ≥1 comorbidity = hospice candidate per Medicare); life expectancy ~1-2 years at FAST 7
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — advanced dementia care principles. Comfort-focused care. Careful hand-feeding + NO tube feeding consensus. Hospice eligibility evaluation. Disease trajectory understanding. RN role: aligned care + family education + symptom management + dignity preservation.

opts.text: MEDICATION REVIEW + DEPRESCRIBING per AGS Beers + STOPP-START — donepezil + memantine continued (modest BPSD); atorvastatin D/C (advanced dementia + limited life expectancy + relaxed goals + reduced pill burden); haloperidol PRN D/C (black-box + EPS); lorazepam PRN D/C (Beers + falls + delirium); sertraline + trazodone + acetaminophen + multivitamin + vit D + calcium continued; pharmacy + geriatric medicine consultative
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — medication review + deprescribing per goals of care + life expectancy + Beers + STOPP-START. Reduces pill burden + side effects + cost. Improves safety + quality of life. Pharmacy + geriatric medicine consultative.

opts.text: Allergies: sulfa (rash) + fluoxetine (severe agitation 2 yr ago — documented contraindication)
opts.rat: Reassuring — allergies documented. Avoid sulfa + fluoxetine. Other SSRIs (sertraline current) OK if needed. Not the priority cue at this moment but important for safe medication management.

opts.text: Administer HALOPERIDOL 5 mg IM immediately for the 1045 agitation + door-seeking behavior because the patient is at risk of injury and antipsychotics are first-line for dementia agitation
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach. Antipsychotics are NOT first-line for BPSD + carry FDA BLACK-BOX warning for INCREASED MORTALITY in elderly with dementia. Haloperidol 5 mg is also a HIGH DOSE for elderly (typical dose 0.25–1 mg if absolutely needed). FIRST-LINE = non-pharmacologic + DICE workup + addressing unmet needs (this patient's de-escalation was effective with calm voice + warm tea + reminiscence + toileting — no medication needed). If antipsychotic ever needed for severe agitation + risk of harm = LOW-DOSE quetiapine preferred over haloperidol (lower EPS + sedation manageable).

opts.text: Apply 4-point physical restraints to prevent the patient from leaving the unit + going into other residents' rooms; the patient is a wandering risk + safety hazard to others
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach + violates dementia care standards + dignity. PHYSICAL RESTRAINTS are not appropriate in dementia care + WORSEN agitation + cause harm (injury, decubitus ulcer, contractures, respiratory compromise, death from positional asphyxia) + violate dignity + are RESTRICTED by federal regulations (CMS — least restrictive interventions + only with documented assessment + alternatives tried + time-limited + family consent in dementia). ALTERNATIVES = environmental (locked unit + circular path + safety design), non-pharm de-escalation, validation + redirection, person-centered approach, addressing unmet needs. Restraints reserved for true emergencies (acute risk of serious self-injury) with extensive documentation + alternatives + time-limited.

steps.title: Analyze Cues
steps.sub: What dementia care + BPSD + Comfort principles apply?
steps.inst: Select ALL accurate linkages between cues and underlying principles.

opts.text: BPSD FRAMEWORK: behavioral + psychological symptoms of dementia present in 90%+ of patients; agitation + wandering + sundowning + sleep disturbance + ADL refusal + hallucinations + delusions + mood disturbance + sexual disinhibition; DRIVEN BY UNMET NEEDS not 'difficult behavior'; PERSON-CENTERED care + DICE framework + non-pharm-first foundational approach
opts.rat: CORRECT — BPSD framework. Behavioral manifestations of dementia. Driven by unmet needs. Person-centered + DICE + non-pharm-first. RN role: assessment + intervention + dignity preservation.

opts.text: DICE FRAMEWORK = (1) DESCRIBE precisely (what + when + where + with whom + frequency + triggers + consequences); (2) INVESTIGATE — medical (pain + UTI + pneumonia + constipation + dehydration + hunger + sleep + medication + delirium), environmental (noise + lighting + temperature + stimulation + unfamiliarity + changes), psychological (loneliness + boredom + frustration + fear + unmet need for purpose); (3) CREATE non-pharm-first plan; (4) EVALUATE + iterate
opts.rat: CORRECT — DICE framework. Systematic approach. American Geriatrics Society + International Psychogeriatric Association consensus. Non-pharm first. RN role: facilitate workup + intervention + iteration.

opts.text: NON-PHARMACOLOGIC FIRST-LINE: address physical needs first (toilet + hydration + nutrition + pain + comfort + sleep + temperature); consistent caregivers + routine + environment; validation therapy (acknowledge feeling not facts); reminiscence + music + pet therapy + simple activities + light therapy; family + memory boxes + photos + familiar objects; simplified communication; bedtime ritual; fall prevention environment; dignity + person-centered care
opts.rat: CORRECT — non-pharm-first framework. Multiple modalities address unmet needs + provide comfort + preserve dignity. Foundation of dementia care. RN role: assess + intervene + advocate + escalate.

opts.text: ANTIPSYCHOTIC FDA BLACK-BOX WARNING all typical + atypical for INCREASED MORTALITY in elderly with dementia-related psychosis (~1.6× over 6–12 weeks; CVA + sedation + falls + EPS + ortho hypo + QT + metabolic); RESERVED FOR severe agitation + risk of harm to self or others NOT responsive to non-pharm + addressing unmet needs; PREFERRED LOW-DOSE QUETIAPINE (lower D2 → lower EPS); LBD severe sensitivity — NMS risk + irreversible Parkinsonism; AVOID haloperidol + olanzapine + first-gen typicals; 4-week reassessment + taper at 3–6 months
opts.rat: CORRECT — antipsychotic risks + appropriate use framework. FDA black-box warning. Quetiapine preferred for elderly + dementia. LBD severe sensitivity. Alternatives. Reassessment + taper. Frequently tested NCLEX safety topic.

opts.text: PAIN in dementia is UNDER-RECOGNIZED + UNDER-TREATED; PAINAD behavioral scale q4h + with any behavioral change; SCHEDULED acetaminophen 500 mg PO q6h first-line for mild-moderate pain in BPSD (often dramatically improves agitation); topical NSAIDs for localized; low-dose opioid for severe; AVOID oral NSAIDs + meperidine + tramadol in elderly (Beers); pain consideration FIRST in DICE workup
opts.rat: CORRECT — pain in dementia framework. PAINAD + scheduled acetaminophen + topical for localized + low-dose opioid for severe + avoidances per Beers. Pain treatment often dramatically improves BPSD. Pain consideration FIRST in DICE.

opts.text: ADVANCED dementia COMFORT-FOCUSED CARE — careful hand-feeding with respect for cues + dignity + safety; NO TUBE FEEDING per multi-society consensus (AGS + ASBH + AAHPM) — does NOT improve outcomes + increases aspiration + complications + reduces quality of life; favorite foods + small bites + supervised + time + patience; oral care q2-4h with foam swabs + lip balm; HOSPICE eligibility FAST 7+ + ≥1 comorbidity (aspiration pneumonia + pressure injury 3-4 + UTI + decline) per Medicare
opts.rat: CORRECT — advanced dementia comfort-focused care framework. Careful hand-feeding + no tube feeding. Hospice eligibility. Multi-society consensus. RN role: comfort care + family education + symptom management + dignity preservation.

opts.text: MEDICATION REVIEW + DEPRESCRIBING per AGS Beers + STOPP-START — goals of care + life expectancy + benefits-risks; reduce anticholinergic burden (worsens cognition + delirium + falls); reduce sedatives/benzodiazepines (Beers); review NSAIDs + sliding-scale insulin alone + anticoagulation + BP medications + cholinesterase inhibitors + memantine + PRN psychotropics; deprescribe atorvastatin in advanced dementia + relaxed goals + limited life expectancy; pharmacy + geriatric medicine consultative
opts.rat: CORRECT — deprescribing framework. Goals of care + life expectancy + Beers + STOPP-START. Reduce pill burden + side effects + cost. Improve safety + quality of life. RN role: facilitate review + advocate + monitor + family education.

opts.text: Antipsychotics are first-line for BPSD because they directly target the agitation + psychotic symptoms in dementia; the FDA black-box warning is overstated + should not affect prescribing decisions
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. NON-PHARMACOLOGIC INTERVENTIONS are FIRST-LINE for BPSD per AGS + International Psychogeriatric Association + AAFP + clinical guidelines. Antipsychotics carry FDA BLACK-BOX WARNING for INCREASED MORTALITY (~1.6×) + CVA + sedation/falls + EPS — REAL CLINICAL CONSIDERATION. Reserve antipsychotics for severe agitation + risk of harm not responsive to non-pharm + addressing unmet needs first. 4-week reassessment + plan to taper. Informed consent + family education.

opts.text: TUBE FEEDING (PEG or NG) should be initiated in this advanced dementia patient because she is losing weight and at risk of malnutrition + aspiration; tube feeding will improve outcomes
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. TUBE FEEDING NOT RECOMMENDED in advanced dementia per AGS + ASBH + AAHPM multi-society consensus — DOES NOT improve survival, function, pressure injury healing, infection risk, or quality of life. INCREASES aspiration pneumonia + agitation needing restraints + distress + pulled tubes + medical complications. CAREFUL HAND-FEEDING with respect for cues + dignity + safety (positioning, small bites, supervised, time + patience, favorite foods) is the standard. Advance directive respect. Family education critical. CONTRAINDICATED ethically + clinically + per patient's prior wishes.

opts.text: Restraints (physical or chemical) should be used routinely in dementia care to prevent wandering + agitation + injury
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach. RESTRAINTS are NOT appropriate in routine dementia care + WORSEN agitation + cause harm (injury + decubitus + contractures + respiratory compromise + dignity violation + death from positional asphyxia) + are RESTRICTED by CMS regulations (least restrictive + alternatives tried + time-limited + family consent + extensive documentation). PHYSICAL restraints reserved for true emergencies. CHEMICAL restraints (sedating medications for behavior control) similarly restricted. ALTERNATIVES = environmental (locked unit + circular path + safe design), non-pharm de-escalation, validation + redirection, person-centered + DICE approach + addressing unmet needs.

steps.title: Prioritize Hypotheses
steps.sub: Rank nursing concerns for this patient's care
steps.inst: Rank these nursing concerns from #1 (HIGHEST priority) to #5 (LOWEST).

opts.text: BPSD Management + Non-Pharm-First + DICE Framework r/t advanced Alzheimer's FAST 6e with progressively severe agitation + wandering + sundowning + sleep disturbance + ADL refusal over past 6 weeks; DICE workup (describe + investigate + create + evaluate); non-pharm interventions (validation + reminiscence + music + family + familiar objects + bedtime ritual + sundowning prep + addressing unmet needs); PAINAD q4h + scheduled acetaminophen
opts.rat: #1 — HIGHEST. BPSD is the primary clinical concern + driving most care decisions. Non-pharm-first + DICE framework + person-centered care address root causes + preserve dignity + avoid antipsychotic risks. Foundation for ongoing care.

opts.text: Antipsychotic Safety + Black-Box Warning r/t FDA BLACK-BOX warning for INCREASED MORTALITY in elderly with dementia-related psychosis (~1.6×) + CVA + sedation + falls + EPS; PRN haloperidol replaced with PRN quetiapine 12.5–25 mg PO HS (lower EPS); only if severe agitation + risk of harm not responsive to non-pharm + addressing unmet needs first; 4-week reassessment + plan to taper at 3–6 months; alternatives = SSRI/citalopram + trazodone (continued) + gabapentin
opts.rat: #2 — HIGH. Antipsychotic safety is critical given black-box warning + significant risks. Reserved for severe agitation not responsive to non-pharm. Quetiapine preferred. Frequently tested NCLEX safety topic.

opts.text: Advance Care Planning + Goals of Care + Hospice Evaluation r/t advanced Alzheimer's FAST 6e progressing toward 7 with hospice eligibility evaluation quarterly + with decline; POLST = DNR/DNI/AND + no artificial nutrition/hydration unless symptom relief + comfort-focused + no hospital transfer; family meeting today reaffirmed goals + medication deprescribing; NO TUBE FEEDING per consensus; CAREFUL HAND-FEEDING with respect
opts.rat: #3 — HIGH. Advance care planning + goals of care alignment essential in advanced dementia. POLST + family partnership + comfort-focused care + appropriate de-escalation of interventions + hospice planning. Foundation for ongoing care + ethical clarity.

opts.text: Family Partnership + Caregiver Support r/t daughter (healthcare proxy) primary advocate + weekly visits + daily phone calls + caregiver support group × 6 months; family meeting today with attending + RN + chaplain + social worker; chaplain spiritual support; bereavement preparation; daughter receptive + supportive of non-pharm + comfort-focused care; reassurance + meaning-making + decision-making support
opts.rat: #4 — Significant + ongoing. Family-centered care + caregiver support are central in dementia. Daughter engagement + emotional support + decision-making partnership. Chaplain + social work + bereavement preparation. Foundation for sustainable family role.

opts.text: Comprehensive Comfort + Function r/t nutrition (careful hand-feeding + favorite foods + hydration), pain (PAINAD + scheduled acetaminophen + topical), sleep (trazodone HS + bedtime ritual), skin (Braden q week + pressure redistribution + repositioning), falls (low bed + bed alarm + chair alarm + supervised path), activity (music + pet therapy + reminiscence + light therapy); medication review + deprescribing (atorvastatin D/C + haloperidol D/C + lorazepam D/C); routine + dignity + person-centered
opts.rat: #5 — Ongoing + foundational. Comprehensive comfort + function across all domains. Multiple coordinated interventions. Dignity + person-centered care. Foundation for daily care + quality of life.

steps.title: Generate Solutions
steps.inst: For EACH intervention, classify as INDICATED or NOT INDICATED for this advanced dementia patient.

opts.text: DICE FRAMEWORK for BPSD: Describe behavior precisely + Investigate underlying causes (pain + UTI + constipation + dehydration + hunger + sleep + medication + environmental + caregiver) + Create non-pharm-first plan + Evaluate response + iterate; PAINAD q4h + with any behavioral change; medical workup including UA + CBC + CMP + medication review + abdominal exam
opts.rat: Standard systematic approach to BPSD per AGS + International Psychogeriatric Association. Identifies modifiable causes + supports non-pharm-first management. Foundation of dementia care.

opts.text: NON-PHARMACOLOGIC FIRST-LINE: address physical needs (toilet + hydration + nutrition + pain + comfort + sleep + temperature); consistent caregivers + routine + environment; validation therapy + reminiscence + music + pet therapy + light therapy; family + memory boxes + photos + familiar objects from home; simplified communication + bedtime ritual + sundowning prep; person-centered care + dignity
opts.rat: Foundational dementia care. Multiple modalities address unmet needs. Person-centered. Most effective initial approach. Evidence-based + recommended by all major guidelines.

opts.text: SCHEDULED acetaminophen 500 mg PO q6h (max 3 g/day in elderly + LFT monitoring) — first-line pain in BPSD often dramatically improves agitation; PAINAD scale q4h + with behavioral change; topical NSAIDs (diclofenac gel) for localized joint pain; low-dose opioid (morphine 2.5 mg PO/SQ q4h PRN) for severe pain not controlled with acetaminophen — with bowel regimen
opts.rat: Pain management in dementia is often under-recognized + driver of BPSD. Scheduled acetaminophen first-line + trial × 2 weeks. Topical NSAIDs alternative. Low-dose opioid for severe with bowel regimen. AVOID oral NSAIDs + meperidine + tramadol per Beers.

opts.text: PRN QUETIAPINE 12.5–25 mg PO HS ONLY if severe agitation + risk of harm to self or others NOT RESPONSIVE TO non-pharm + addressing unmet needs first; FDA black-box warning informed consent + 4-week reassessment + plan to taper at 3–6 months; AVOID haloperidol (high EPS + sedation) + olanzapine (anticholinergic + metabolic + sedating); replaces previous haloperidol PRN
opts.rat: Quetiapine preferred for elderly + dementia (lower D2 → lower EPS) when antipsychotic necessary. Reserved for severe agitation + risk of harm not responsive to non-pharm. Replaces less safe haloperidol PRN. Standard appropriate use with safeguards.

opts.text: Family meeting with daughter (healthcare proxy) + attending + RN + chaplain + social worker; reaffirm POLST (DNR/DNI/AND + no artificial nutrition + comfort-focused + no hospital transfer); discuss BPSD management + antipsychotic risks vs non-pharm + medication deprescribing + advance care + hospice evaluation; family education + emotional support + chaplain spiritual support; daughter in caregiver support group
opts.rat: Family-centered care + advance care planning + interdisciplinary coordination. Healthcare proxy partnership. Comprehensive discussion of options + values + goals. Spiritual + emotional support. Foundation for ongoing care.

opts.text: MEDICATION REVIEW + DEPRESCRIBING per AGS Beers + STOPP-START — D/C atorvastatin (advanced dementia + limited life expectancy + relaxed goals); D/C haloperidol PRN (black-box + EPS); D/C lorazepam PRN (Beers + falls + delirium); continue donepezil + memantine + sertraline + trazodone + acetaminophen + multivitamin + vit D + calcium + lisinopril + amlodipine
opts.rat: Deprescribing per goals of care + life expectancy + Beers + STOPP-START. Reduces pill burden + side effects + cost. Improves safety + quality of life. Pharmacy + geriatric medicine consultative.

opts.text: HALOPERIDOL 5 mg IM immediately for the 1045 agitation + door-seeking behavior; antipsychotics are first-line for dementia agitation + this patient is at risk of harm to herself + others
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. Antipsychotics are NOT first-line for BPSD. FDA BLACK-BOX warning for INCREASED MORTALITY in elderly with dementia + sedation + falls + EPS + ortho hypo. 5 mg is HIGH DOSE for elderly (typical 0.25–1 mg if absolutely needed). NON-PHARM-FIRST (DICE framework) was effective (de-escalation with calm voice + warm tea + reminiscence + toileting within 12 min). If antipsychotic ever needed = LOW-DOSE quetiapine (lower EPS) preferred over haloperidol.

opts.text: 4-point physical restraints to prevent wandering + agitation + intrusion into other residents' rooms
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach + violates dementia care standards + dignity + federal regulation. RESTRAINTS WORSEN agitation + cause harm (injury + decubitus + contractures + respiratory compromise + positional asphyxia death) + violate CMS restraint-free care expectations + dignity. ALTERNATIVES = environmental (locked unit + circular path + safe design + chair alarm + bed alarm), non-pharm de-escalation, validation + redirection, person-centered + addressing unmet needs. Reserved for true emergencies.

opts.text: Initiate NG tube feeding to provide nutrition + hydration given gradual weight loss + decreased appetite + advanced dementia
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. TUBE FEEDING NOT RECOMMENDED in advanced dementia per AGS + ASBH + AAHPM multi-society consensus — does NOT improve survival, function, pressure injury healing, infection risk, or quality of life. INCREASES aspiration pneumonia + agitation needing restraints + distress + pulled tubes + complications. CAREFUL HAND-FEEDING with respect for cues + dignity + safety is the standard. Advance directive respect. Family education critical.

opts.text: CAREFUL HAND-FEEDING with respect for cues + dignity + safety (positioning, small bites, supervised, time + patience, favorite foods); oral care q2-4h with foam swabs + saline + lip balm (avoid glycerin); hydration 1500–2000 mL/day via various forms (water + juice + tea + soup + popsicles + ice chips); weekly weights + UOP + dehydration signs
opts.rat: Standard advanced dementia nutrition + hydration + oral care. Person-centered + dignity + comfort-focused. Multi-society consensus on hand-feeding > tube feeding. Family education + reassurance.

opts.text: ENVIRONMENTAL non-pharm care: familiar objects from home (favorite quilt + photos + grandfather clock with reduced chime volume); consistent caregivers in transition periods; routine + structured day; validation therapy + reminiscence (teaching career) + music (1950s big band) + favorite foods; sundowning prep 1430+ (gradual dim lighting + reduced stimulation + family room + bedtime ritual approach); fall prevention environment (low bed + bed alarm + chair alarm + circular path supervised); chaplain + social work + activity therapy + pet therapy
opts.rat: Comprehensive environmental + non-pharm care. Foundation of person-centered dementia care. Multiple modalities address unmet needs + comfort + dignity. Standard evidence-based approach.

opts.text: INDWELLING Foley catheter to manage urinary incontinence + reduce wandering for toileting + simplify care; long-term placement appropriate in advanced dementia
opts.rat: ⚠️ NOT INDICATED + worsens outcomes. Foley catheters in dementia INCREASE UTIs (CAUTI), agitation (foreign body + restraint-like sensation), falls (tether), restraint-like effect on dignity, infection-driven delirium → worsening BPSD, hospital transfer. PROGRAMMED VOIDING (regular timed toileting + every 2-3 hr + cue + assist) is the standard for incontinence in dementia. CAUTI bundle: catheter only if absolutely indicated + daily necessity review + remove ASAP. Long-term placement in advanced dementia almost never indicated.

steps.title: Take Action
steps.sub: FIRST action sequence at this 1045 incident + ongoing care
steps.inst: Rank the order in which the RN should implement these actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: DE-ESCALATE the 1045 agitation episode WITH NON-PHARM interventions: approach calmly with calm voice + offer warm tea + reminiscence about teaching career + validation therapy (acknowledge feelings) + gentle redirection from fire exit + offer toileting (urgency suspected) + ensure safety of patient + other residents; document behavior + interventions + response
opts.rat: #1 — FIRST. Immediate safety + de-escalation. Non-pharm interventions effective within 12 min. RN role: rapid response + safety + non-pharm intervention + documentation. Avoid antipsychotic + restraint.

opts.text: DICE WORKUP — Describe behavior precisely + Investigate underlying causes (UA + CBC + CMP + medication review + abdominal exam + PAINAD); identify triggers (shift change + new caregiver + breakfast timing + clock chime + urinary urgency); Create non-pharm plan + Evaluate response
opts.rat: #2 — Second. Systematic workup identifies modifiable triggers + unmet needs. Foundation for ongoing intervention. Medical workup rules out delirium + acute illness. RN role: facilitate + document + escalate.

opts.text: ENVIRONMENTAL preparation for sundowning + bedtime: consistent caregivers in afternoon + clock chime volume reduced + bedtime ritual maintained + sundowning prep (gradual dim lighting + reduced stimulation + soft music + favorite quilt + family room + reminiscence + family phone call); supper served early at 1530 with favorite foods; PAINAD assessed + scheduled acetaminophen given on time
opts.rat: #3 — Third. Preventive environmental modifications + bedtime ritual + sundowning prep + nutrition + pain management. Addresses anticipated needs + reduces likelihood of further BPSD.

opts.text: FAMILY MEETING at 1500 with daughter + attending physician + Memory Care RN + chaplain + social worker: review BPSD progression + DICE workup + non-pharm care + antipsychotic risks + medication deprescribing + advance care + hospice eligibility evaluation; reaffirm POLST + comfort-focused goals; chaplain spiritual support + social work caregiver resources; updated POLST signed
opts.rat: #4 — Fourth. Family-centered care + interdisciplinary coordination + advance care planning. Healthcare proxy partnership. Comprehensive discussion. Reaffirm + update goals. Foundation for ongoing care.

opts.text: ONGOING CARE — medication deprescribing implemented (atorvastatin D/C + haloperidol D/C + lorazepam D/C + add quetiapine PRN); behavior charting + DICE framework documentation; weekly multidisciplinary care planning meetings; monthly family check-in calls + daily phone calls + Saturday visits; quarterly hospice eligibility reassessment + sooner with decline; staff education + person-centered care training + DICE training + non-pharm interventions
opts.rat: #5 — Ongoing + foundational. Sustainable system + interdisciplinary care + family partnership + staff education + ongoing reassessment. Foundation for safe + dignified + comfort-focused care over the course of dementia trajectory.

steps.title: Evaluate Outcomes
steps.inst: For each finding, classify the trajectory.

opts.text: Day 0 end of shift 1900: 1045 incident de-escalated within 12 min with non-pharm interventions (calm voice + warm tea + reminiscence + toileting); UA clean = no UTI; abdominal soft = no constipation; PAINAD 1/10 = no significant pain; sundowning prep 1430+ effective with gradual dim lighting + reduced stimulation + family room + reminiscence; sundowning episode 1700–1830 de-escalated with validation therapy + presence + warm milk; no PRN medications used; family meeting 1500 productive + daughter supportive; updated POLST signed
opts.rat: Excellent shift outcome — multiple BPSD episodes de-escalated with non-pharm interventions + no antipsychotic needed + family meeting productive + advance care plan reaffirmed. Standard expected best-case outcome with effective person-centered care + DICE framework + family partnership.

opts.text: Day 7 (1 week): non-pharm interventions sustained; behavioral incidents reduced (2 minor incidents this week vs ~5-7 per week previously); no PRN quetiapine used; sleep stable with bedtime ritual + trazodone HS; nutrition stable (eating 50-75% with hand-feeding assistance + favorite foods); weight stable at 52 kg; daughter visit Saturday positive; no falls; no skin breakdown; no acute illness; routine + structured day maintained; chaplain weekly visit + music therapy weekly
opts.rat: Standard expected sustained positive trajectory with effective person-centered + DICE + non-pharm-first care. Behavioral incidents reduced. Family + interdisciplinary engagement. Comfort-focused + dignity-preserving.

opts.text: Day 14 — patient develops acute UTI with fever 38.5°C + new agitation worsening + confusion fluctuating + decreased PO intake; CAM positive for DELIRIUM SUPERIMPOSED ON DEMENTIA (DSD); UA + UC positive for E. coli; ceftriaxone 1 g IV daily × 7 days for community-acquired pyelonephritis in nursing facility; non-pharm delirium prevention bundle (HELP — orientation + hearing/vision + mobility + hydration/nutrition + sleep + family + avoid Foley + avoid restraints + address pain + medication review); avoiding hospitalization (POLST = no hospital transfer); IV fluids + monitoring + symptom support
opts.rat: DELIRIUM SUPERIMPOSED ON DEMENTIA (DSD) from acute UTI — common cause of acute BPSD worsening. Workup + treatment + non-pharm delirium prevention bundle. Avoid hospital transfer per POLST. RN role: recognize delirium pattern + workup + treat underlying + supportive care. Standard expected complication of advanced dementia + appropriate response.

opts.text: Day 21 — DSD resolved with treatment of UTI; behavioral baseline restored; weight rebounded to 53 kg with improved appetite + hydration; cognitive baseline (FAST 6e) maintained without permanent decline despite delirium episode; family relieved + chaplain visit + reaffirmed goals; no antipsychotic used throughout; non-pharm care continues effective
opts.rat: Standard recovery from DSD episode. UTI treated + behavioral baseline restored + no permanent cognitive decline (favorable outcome — DSD often accelerates decline but appropriate care prevented). Non-pharm care continued effective + no antipsychotic. Family + interdisciplinary engagement sustained.

opts.text: Day 90 (3 months): patient has progressed to FAST 7a (limited vocabulary 6 words) + minimal participation in activities + increased sleep + occasional refusal to eat; weight down 2 kg over 3 months; pressure injury Stage 1 on coccyx (developing despite repositioning); recurrent UTI #3 in 6 months + recent fever after antibiotics; HOSPICE eligibility evaluation = MEETS criteria (FAST 7a + recurrent UTIs + pressure injury + weight loss + decline trajectory); family meeting + daughter agrees to hospice election; Medicare hospice benefit activated + interdisciplinary hospice team + bereavement preparation
opts.rat: Standard expected disease progression in advanced dementia. FAST 7a + comorbidities + decline trajectory = hospice eligible per Medicare. Family meeting + hospice election + comprehensive interdisciplinary care. RN role: recognize trajectory + facilitate hospice + family support + bereavement preparation.
