opts.text: Day 240 (8 months): patient peacefully passed at Memory Care Unit at 0445 with daughter at bedside + chaplain present + favorite quilt + soft music playing + final hours of comfort-focused care with low-dose morphine for breathing comfort + glycopyrrolate for terminal secretions + family presence; advance directives honored; no hospital transfer; RN pronouncement per state hospice protocol; bereavement services begin per Medicare hospice 13-month benefit
opts.rat: A 'good death' — comfort-focused + dignity-preserving + family-present + meaningful environment + advance directives honored + symptom-managed + spiritual support. Standard ideal end-of-life outcome for advanced dementia with appropriate comfort-focused care + family partnership. Bereavement services + grief support continued for family.

opts.text: Day 14 alternative — RN administers haloperidol 5 mg IM emergent for severe agitation that was NOT first managed with DICE framework + non-pharm interventions; patient develops severe EPS + Parkinsonism + sedation + fall with hip fracture within 48 hr; family distressed + clinical nurse educator + medication safety review + protocol revision + staff education on antipsychotic risks + non-pharm-first + DICE framework
opts.rat: ⚠️ HALOPERIDOL INAPPROPRIATELY USED + harmful outcomes. Failed to apply DICE framework + non-pharm-first. High dose for elderly. EPS + sedation + fall + fracture = serious harm + cascade of consequences. Just culture + medication safety review + protocol revision + staff education. Standard adverse response demonstrating WHY antipsychotic black-box warning + non-pharm-first matter.

opts.text: Day 30 staff debriefing + team well-being: experienced staff member shares emotional toll of caring for advanced dementia patients with progressive decline + behavioral challenges; EAP + peer support + chaplain availability + meaning-making + self-care reinforced; team huddle + recognition of compassionate care + protocol effectiveness + family gratitude + person-centered approach success; staff retention + sustainability supported
opts.rat: Standard team well-being + sustainability practice. Compassion fatigue in dementia care recognized + supported. EAP + peer support + chaplain + meaning-making essential. Foundation for sustained compassionate person-centered care. Not improving or worsening — ongoing institutional culture practice.

opts.text: Day 60 alternative — RN identifies during chart audit that PRN haloperidol is still on chart from prior orders + not deprescribed per family meeting decision; RN escalates to provider + medication reconciliation completed + haloperidol PRN D/C order entered + quetiapine PRN added per family meeting plan; pharmacy + geriatric medicine consultation + chart corrected; just culture + near-miss reporting + system improvement
opts.rat: EXCELLENT RN advocacy + medication safety culture. Identifying gap between family meeting decision + chart orders + escalating to provider + correcting. Just culture + near-miss reporting + system improvement. RN role in safety + advocacy + institutional improvement. Protective response to identified gap.

opts.text: 1-year family bereavement follow-up: daughter doing well in grief + caregiver group continued + adapted to loss + grateful for compassionate person-centered care + hospice support; mother's life celebrated + photos + memorial; bereavement services per Medicare 13-month benefit + monthly contacts + commemoration; daughter expressed plan to volunteer with Alzheimer's Association + advocacy work + meaningful legacy
opts.rat: Excellent long-term bereavement outcome — meaningful grief + adaptation + advocacy + legacy. Demonstrates value of comprehensive compassionate person-centered + family-engaged + comfort-focused care throughout dementia trajectory + into end-of-life + bereavement. Standard ideal trajectory.

title: Intentional Acetaminophen Overdose ~25 g + Concomitant Alcohol 12 Hours Ago in 38-Year-Old Woman with Major Depressive Disorder Post-Relationship Conflict: 21-Hour IV N-Acetylcysteine Protocol, Rumack-Matthew Nomogram Interpretation, King's College Criteria for Liver Transplant Referral, Psychiatric Hold + Safety Planning + Capacity Assessment, and Family-Centered Communication
subtitle: ACETAMINOPHEN OVERDOSE = leading cause of acute liver failure in US (~50% of cases — 40% intentional + 60% unintentional/staggered); TOXIC DOSE >150 mg/kg single ingestion or >7.5 g in adult (LOWER threshold with chronic alcohol + malnutrition + fasting + isoniazid + CYP2E1-inducing drugs); 4 CLINICAL STAGES = Stage I 0–24 hr (minimal/nonspecific N/V; labs often normal); Stage II 24–72 hr (RUQ pain + AST/ALT rise + INR rise + 'silent period' as patient may feel better); Stage III 72–96 hr (PEAK hepatotoxicity + coagulopathy + encephalopathy + AKI + hypoglycemia + lactic acidosis + cerebral edema); Stage IV >96 hr (recovery OR death OR transplant); MECHANISM = acetaminophen normally metabolized by glucuronidation + sulfation; small fraction via CYP2E1 → NAPQI (toxic intermediate) → conjugated by GLUTATHIONE; OVERDOSE depletes glutathione → free NAPQI causes HEPATOCYTE NECROSIS centrilobular; NAC (N-ACETYLCYSTEINE) = GLUTATHIONE PRECURSOR + direct NAPQI scavenger + improves microcirculation + antioxidant; IV PRESCOTT/SMILKSTEIN 21-HR PROTOCOL = 150 mg/kg in 200 mL D5W loading over 60 min → 50 mg/kg in 500 mL D5W over 4 hr → 100 mg/kg in 1000 mL D5W over 16 hr = total 300 mg/kg over 21 hr; ORAL 72-hr protocol = 140 mg/kg loading → 70 mg/kg q4h × 17 doses (less preferred — vomiting + longer); CONTINUE NAC beyond 21 hr if INR >2 + AST/ALT >1000 + clinical hepatotoxicity (extended protocol until INR <2 + AST/ALT downtrending); MOST EFFECTIVE within 8–10 hr of ingestion (reduces hepatotoxicity to <5%); STILL BENEFICIAL up to 24–72 hr + even with established acute liver failure (improves outcomes per Prescott + Smilkstein); GIVE NAC BEFORE LEVEL RESULT if presentation >8 hr + high suspicion (don't wait for level); RUMACK-MATTHEW NOMOGRAM = serum acetaminophen level at ≥4 hr post-ingestion plotted on time-concentration; TREATMENT LINE at 150 μg/mL at 4 hr; treat if AT OR ABOVE line; nomogram DOES NOT APPLY to staggered + chronic + unknown-time + sustained-release + extended-release ingestion; KING'S COLLEGE CRITERIA for liver transplant referral in acetaminophen-induced ALF (POOR PROGNOSIS without transplant): arterial pH <7.3 after fluid resuscitation OR (INR >6.5 + Cr >3.4 + Grade III/IV hepatic encephalopathy); sensitivity ~70% + specificity ~90%; lactate >3.5 after fluid resuscitation OR >3.0 at 12 hr = adverse + transplant marker; UNOS STATUS 1A for acute liver failure = highest priority for transplant; PSYCHIATRIC = SUICIDE ATTEMPT requires safety + 1:1 sitter + lethal means restriction (lock up medications + remove sharps + suicide-resistant room) + 72-hr hold (CA 5150) if not voluntary + capacity assessment + multidisciplinary psychiatric consult + safety planning (Stanley-Brown brief intervention) + post-medical inpatient psychiatric care vs outpatient with safety net; COMPLICATIONS of ALF = CEREBRAL EDEMA (cause of death in 30% — ICP monitoring + mannitol + hypertonic saline + hyperventilation + therapeutic hypothermia + sedation); COAGULOPATHY (do NOT correct INR with FFP unless active bleeding or planned procedure — INR trend is prognostic + transplant criterion; vitamin K minimal effect in ALF as synthesis impaired); HYPOGLYCEMIA (glucose 10% IV); AKI (CRRT preferred over IHD for hemodynamic stability + ICP); INFECTION (broad-spectrum coverage + low threshold to treat — high infection rate + worsens outcomes); FAMILY-centered communication + ethics consult + capacity + transplant decision-making for intentional self-harm patient (treated equitably — no historical discrimination)

patient.name: J. Patel
patient.code: Full code; advance directives not on file; healthcare proxy = spouse (married 8 yr, currently estranged after relationship conflict that precipitated this event); capacity assessment ongoing (decisional currently per geriatric medicine + psychiatry consult — alert + oriented + understanding of situation + appropriate communication despite suicide attempt); family + parents notified + en route from out of state
patient.allergies: Penicillin (rash per chart, 5 yr ago — never reproduced); no other documented allergies
patient.admitDate: Arrived at ED 1830 via ambulance after spouse returned home at 1745 to find empty extra-strength acetaminophen 500 mg bottle (50 tablets) + open bottle of red wine + ~1/2 bottle remaining + patient lying on couch crying + verbalizing 'I just want it all to stop'; ingestion per patient stated to occur 'about 6 hours ago' = approximately 1230 today (12 hr before ED arrival; staggered with wine over 2 hr possibly); ESTIMATED dose ~25 g acetaminophen (50 × 500 mg = 25 g = ~340 mg/kg in 73 kg patient — MASSIVELY toxic dose; toxic threshold >150 mg/kg); EtOH co-ingestion potentiates initial absorption + CNS but chronic alcohol use (which patient does NOT have per history) is what increases hepatotoxicity risk through CYP2E1 induction; admitted to medicine ICU Bed 5 from ED at 2100 after initial stabilization + NAC loading dose + psychiatric consult + 5150 involuntary 72-hr hold initiated for safety
